name: James =?UTF-8?B?T8Oi4oKs4oSiS2VlZmU=?=
subject: Dark Nightmares
body:
I've been having nightmares recently that are dark beyond description. Terrifying. One occurred at 3:30 in the morning on the day that Charlie was killed. In one nightmare, a landline phone next to my bed rang. I answered, and on the other end was some demon, the most haunting voice I've heard in my life, saying, "I'm in your basement."

| | [****](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zeqgdjcijhqp86p9eu2lr5vvd2hnpg5cj8kh061g_rp22sh2s8i6eor3c3gm4oj5clh2bs) [****](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zsdulp1e3vmuq4egjafj3tn64kpmodo3phh4fe70_rp22sh2s8i6eor3c3gm4oj5clh2bs) Dear Patriot,

I've been having nightmares recently that are dark beyond description. Terrifying. One occurred at 3:30 in the morning on the day that Charlie was killed.

In one nightmare, a landline phone next to my bed rang. I answered, and on the other end was **some demon** , the most haunting voice I've heard in my life, saying, "**I'm in your basement.** "

I sat up immediately, not knowing if it was real or a dream. My hands ran alongside my bed to feel the fabric in the darkness. It took some time to recognize that I was awake. I looked at my nightstand, and there was no phone.

Those who know much more about God and the Devil than I do say that this is an example of spiritual warfare. They tell me that it's meant to terrify me, to make me question myself and my own sanity. **These nightmares, this evil, come just before a story OMG will be breaking that I believe will expose evil in a way we've never seen before**.

The story was placed on hold due to the shock and grief we've all felt for Charlie Kirk. I used to run stories by Charlie to get his feedback. I relayed our next release to Charlie before he was shot. It was the last text Charlie Kirk ever sent me. I prefer to keep the message private, but he understood the significance of the story and that **it strikes at the heart of evil in our nation.**

I'm reluctant to "hype" stories anymore. That's not my intent. But I am asking for your help.

First, I'm humbly asking for your prayers for me and OMG's staff. If you're a believer, I don't need to say anything else, other than "thank you."

Secondly, [**your support is needed**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zvive68h2i02fbb4pbo0mmrso176g5cns7tdohi0_rp22sh2s8i6eor3c3gm4oj5clh2bs). [**Any amount that you can contribute is appreciated**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zcn1bsdavif4gaklrffgtb9761jbfmqk3sro6978_rp22sh2s8i6eor3c3gm4oj5clh2bs).

This story was one of our least expensive to produce, and you'll see why when it comes out. But it will open the floodgates of truth, and we need to be prepared for that. This story has the potential to change the world, but that's not up to me. OMG, reports the truth, the world decides. But I would be negligent in my duties if we're not prepared to deal with a flood of high-level whistleblowers after this release. And that's where your support is needed. [**Please, donate whatever you can today**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zldoif0o5p35d55rcjv5734tub4g9nevu5vd95l8_rp22sh2s8i6eor3c3gm4oj5clh2bs).

Thank you again for all that you do and, as always, stay tuned.

In Truth, James O'Keefe
Founder and CEO
O'Keefe Media Group

---
[****](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z9ls5nv4fq1uqusg7mj20n8avilncogb0k67tdg8_rp22sh2s8i6eor3c3gm4oj5clh2bs)
---

Pop Acta Media | American Action News

"I was surprised to find that the densely packed packages were easy to prepare and were tasty as well. I definitely recommend having survival food on hand for the times when 'life' happens while we are planning other things."_ _**Billy H.**
"Received my food kit, and I was able to make the potato soup on my cookstove. Just needed boiling water. Gave it a good stir and let it simmer. When I opened up the pot, it smelled so good. You can see the chunks of potatoes and carrots in each bite you take. It's a good soup."_ _**Kevin S.**
"Perfect starter kit for any scenario. Put one in your car, work bag, in your house or RV. If you want to start preparing safe food, this is where to start."_ _**Carol B.**
"I have been iced in this weekend. I thought I would try my potato soup. To my utter delight, IT WAS DELICIOUS. Thank you for such a wonderful product and the peace of mind it brings."_ _**Ken K.**
"I am not a full-blown survivalist. I am not an idiot, either. I have been through enough in my life and have seen friends who have been through an emergency situation. Save up if you have to, but get at least a month's supply. It tastes good. Although if it's a dire of an emergency, you will be happy to eat anything. IF you have something to eat for your family. We made some of this product, and had family and friends over for them to taste. They all agreed they didn't think anything would be this good, and they will be ordering. Just do something. You can't miss out on this deal."_ _**Justin A.**
"My wife and I tried the food and we were both surprised about how good it tasted and how satisfied it made us feel afterwards. It feels good knowing that I can provide for my family if a crisis arose and I intend to get more in the future. Also the shipping and customer service has been top notch. body:
We can’t afford to wait for our elections to fix themselves.

[](https://trk.emails.troydowning._A)[](https://trk.emails.troydowning.-MYZAg)Peter,
The integrity of our electoral process is fundamental to the survival of our democracy. Now, one of the world's most influential voices is demanding immediate, commonsense action to ensure every legal vote is counted and election results are trusted.[](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)In his recent statements, the world's richest man laid out four essential safeguards that every American should be able to agree on to ensure election transparency and security:
1. Voter ID to stop fraud before it starts
2. Paper ballots to verify every vote
3. Same-day voting to keep the process secure
4. Proof of citizenship to protect the integrity of every election

These aren’t radical or partisan ideas. They’re simple, commonsense protections that are standard practice in developed democracies worldwide.**
**Now more than ever, securing our elections must be Congress’s top priority. Our majority, the integrity of our democracy, and President Trump’s America First agenda ALL DEPEND on delivering on this urgent voter ID mandate.

| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

Near Trump Tower in Las Vegas, a Tesla Cybertruck exploded—and it’s raising serious questions. | | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | A Tesla Explosion That Changes Everything | Near Trump Tower in Las Vegas, a Tesla Cybertruck exploded—and it’s raising serious questions. # Saturday, December 27, 2025

**Good morning!** On this day in 1968, NASA’s [Apollo 8 mission](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1uwyAUQOGnwVssuIDtDAyVIm_pVlWdLH4uMS0OEdwk8ttX6St0PcM3nM_zcrYpp-vl3W5oKJblis-WkQjr4teaGm22Bbs3qukHWy2uUFuAgz4IOMC4BJvyvqgumIgIKDs0YhyGSUqteLcaIY_TZCOXOGrHcXRe-VdTOgYPR90l89IEwCgE1_LYe6kdDl4MatCWy8AUx5Co1P67pCutGPNeHlh7X7Yum5Xo1ph8YzAzmPdyp3vvkMH8MTlxiKcC5YvJOaKle0UmT221FUNXTbg9U86pDSNT_LLZlP9I-teHh4HfAAAA__8VOnEV) splashed down in the Pacific, completing the first crewed mission to orbit the Moon. Two women recreated _The Holiday_ by [swapping homes](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LHOnDAQBOCnMZ1P9hpD_sJFGro_bUrkY5djc8ZG9uoQbx-RV0g30kifRvP7e_6OnDi_fsWdgqxlznS2RCJU52Wr3GSPDePVpPKbWi3PIm0GA15b0DDOGDldc99hWImAXEfBjsPwwznfm24LPY5Lj4CGPA3m-eVxNETOrg49xpE6DrdmAUZrjXdfj8X5Jw2LHfrBR-NQ9YaQpdTHn8JZNlrTVT5UH0vZuxQ2kaMp91PBpGDK11Ga3JWC6YYVTBbu7BRMiVdqciVSMMlGeiuJMV6aa9KZTn2V-qaqY0adSsaSqep2xkMn_lDTWzk1iz4pi4KpqwGPk1PiNoyqN689cvo3Sv7ryU-AvwEAAP__f8yIwA) between Brooklyn and London after a single social media post, as we report below, turning a movie fantasy into a real-life transatlantic adventure that drew millions of viewers along for the ride. Today’s sponsor is [LMNT](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykzzluwzAQQNHTSJ0FarjILlikcee0KQkuI2tiSjTIgQXdPnCQG6T_eMD_urmbp0zb_dOvaHkubsO9ZWTG6uJSqfHqW_JH40oPbLWEws2BAH0a4QSTS57y4VSf7IwIKHu042TMWUqtRL9YLXSa4KJFiAHOIYEKEiIgzOYyG4g92bc2AkzjKLS8DFHqgCaORhnthUydEpiISx2-C2284JyP8sI6xLL22S7Mz9bJjw6uHVz3fR9Spe2R143fRQfXv76vNj13ypmamTol7qun_Ivwv85fFn4CAAD__8wgbdU). ## US Strikes ISIS in Nigeria
U.S. Africa Command confirmed Thursday that [U.S. forces struck multiple ISIS camps](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHOozAQhJ8GOiOwwU4KF9fQ5dor0cZeYO8MjrybIN7-xK__Df5upNHM6Js_j-kBlGhffsOGXuY87XhwQhEsU1gLsWzAEU6WQv-QS35m4Um3elCdVtpNESidU19HPyNqNDX6zll7M2bo23r1zmpjXRvB3edoBjc4BBPx1gfQYAPU5K-2TmvXde1g7k0wwxNt6GxvB2hNrPoWI0kuzd9Mu6w4pzN_sDQhb3Xyq8iLK_Or0mOlx-M4mvDki-LyKz1estLjW7FK8N7Diqy-aVTeFTGxEigLCiva1U4LFgIl5b29FMN5hevi4-uglIitq_p22YDS17786LSP1_8DAAD__8ZKg2M) in northwest Nigeria after militants allegedly targeted and brutally killed primarily Christian civilians. The strikes followed President Trump's warning that "there would be hell to pay" if killings of Christians continued. Officials provided no details on casualties, targets, or the number of strikes. Nigerian officials said they are working with U.S. partners to fight terrorism and protect lives, [acknowledging Islamist violence](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2uozAQhZ8GOiP_AUnhYhu6bLslGuwBZtfYkT0JytuvuLpvcNtzpKPzfX8e8wMoUtp-w4GO1zwnPGtEZiyz3wtVPqAG-FQu9A9ryUvmOmupe6G00OMcgOJntm1wK6JG06JT4zDcjOmtbHenQMqgcUALt_sq7QK4LrpHI61Ff8OW3LWmtB6Vkr25d970Cw5eDXboQZrQWImBOJfub6bEO67xk99YOp-PNrqd-Vkb86vRU6On8zy7tPiL4uqvJJcYGj3BWshDo6dEGxYCAW8sXMUrUQTGAlG8qgDPlJPwOT-xAFPaBFD5phfFJ9C9klK1xYXnSTFSHcbGyu0Ail-P-Eca307_DwAA__-n5oor) and kidnappings in the north while denying systematic persecution of Christians. Nigeria's [population is nearly evenly split](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLFugzAQhp_GbI7wGZt08NCFLV07IoMPcGMw8l0S8fYVVdUX6HbSSZ_-7_u89TcfU9zmD7-i4yn3G74oITOWflxKJF49BX8Ql3hHKnnITD3UYKQCCW0ffExH31TBTYiAukKnWmuvWpumrhbXjKOa9HBtg268AcQBvLFGe6PC6LWtojtpCqBVqjb67TJqM6AdlW2s8bUOoqkxRM7l8pXjxgtO6chPLJcxr1VyC_NOQr8L6AR027Fn4vMloDvBAjoF520FdA-Sp5-Ajstj3SXt6O8k84Nl3uQWZyzRy19XGSmSnDNLL59YDjn4IP-ayL0g4cYCuqq4sL9iSpFsK5p6Xn1MP-v4X0mfDr4DAAD__ylMi_Y) between Muslims in the north and Christians in the south, where a humanitarian group says more than 7,000 Christians were killed this year.
## Biggest Car Trends for 2026 Take Shape
Automakers are lining up an unusually crowded 2026 model year, with nearly [160 new or redesigned vehicles ](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2u3CAQhZ8Gd1gw2DguKLah262i3NLCMF6Ti2EFk7X27SPnFdKMNNLRd36-7svdxRTz8-EONLSVJePZEhJhXfxeY6PDteA-jWr8xlbLWqgtIGDkEjhMS3AxfZahC2ZDBFQdGjlp_UOpcRDdblBIJd2KUq2zBL1OYt2E0EHNHvwgVRfNRZMAk5RiVHPv1bii9lIPenRCBTYIDJFK7X-XmGnHLX3KG2vvy9ElsxO9GlM3BpaBPc-zf6ay4tXjjBUvFQN7vbxiQteQgb0sGVgJ15kYWAVignFmYAUDi5mBfVwExG9-y7n8yR4b_7kj_yo1Bd74vTTit0zRx5cjDPyBJ_-Fe_QJGwcBut_pSF014XXGlGLTExvE83Ax_YtO_7X328DfAAAA__8XnpJ-)expected globally, and many aimed squarely at U.S. buyers. The common thread across [ upcoming launches is flexibility](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGu4yAQAL_G7rBgwfhSUFzjLtdeaWFY23uHIYJNrPz9U94vvH40I83f-3L3lCjvf_yJjreyZLxaQmasSzgqNT59i_7duNJ_bLWshdsCEkahQMC0RE_pvZg-ug0RUPfo1GTtL61HI_vDbZNGq03EsK5GWjXF2xaCNjpGE8Yoe3IfmwKYlJKjvg1BjyvaoKyxo5c6dkZiJC51-Fco84FbepcX1iGUs0_uYH60Tv_uYO5gvq5r8E8uayr7B-hg3tDzs2LrYOYDxVkaC5-ZAj08YxQZL_HCg0LCJkI5Ke-CsgAJtq8uPi5KiZqdOiP301P67vKPZr0cfAUAAP___8yAaA)--more hybrids, refreshed gas models, and carefully targeted EVs--rather than an all-in electric push. On the business side, the U.S. car market is finally [stabilizing after years of turmoil](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2u4yAUhJ_G7rDg8GO7oNjGXbbd0iJwHNhgE8HZWHn7la_uG9x65hvNzJ_benMpp-Px2-1oaSvrgWfLSIR19bGmRrtrwX0a1fTEVsu9UFuBg2YCGIxrcCl_VtUHuyECyh6tGI2ZpNSK99HOZhuDnwWfnTboNSg_KQ_TqMQktZr6ZK80ATAKwbWcBy_1HY0XRhntuAyd4hgSlTr8LemgiFv-lDfWwZe9zzYSvVonf3WwdLCc5zlg2P8doV16B4t3lV2jOlio4hEao-iInSln1qJ7IQMOhl223dUnEvvmWTpaekRqQ6Q999WG1wWlZsZO8cfuUv6qQD_67W3hfwAAAP__L4GDOA), with inventories improving and prices cooling slightly, although affordability remains the biggest hurdle for buyers. Bottom line for 2026: more choices on dealer lots, fewer moonshots, and a sharper focus on what American drivers are actually willing--and able--to buy.
## Life Imitates Rom-Com
Two women recreated the 2006 film _The Holiday_ by [swapping homes across the Atlantic](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LHOnDAQBOCnMZ1P9hpD_sJFGro_bUrkY5djc8ZG9uoQbx-RV0g30kifRvP7e_6OnDi_fsWdgqxlznS2RCJU52Wr3GSPDePVpPKbWi3PIm0GA15b0DDOGDldc99hWImAXEfBjsPwwznfm24LPY5Lj4CGPA3m-eVxNETOrg49xpE6DrdmAUZrjXdfj8X5Jw2LHfrBR-NQ9YaQpdTHn8JZNlrTVT5UH0vZuxQ2kaMp91PBpGDK11Ga3JWC6YYVTBbu7BRMiVdqciVSMMlGeiuJMV6aa9KZTn2V-qaqY0adSsaSqep2xkMn_lDTWzk1iz4pi4KpqwGPk1PiNoyqN689cvo3Sv7ryU-AvwEAAP__f8yIwA), and millions watched it unfold on TikTok. In the Nancy Meyers movie, Cameron Diaz and Kate Winslet trade homes between Los Angeles and the English countryside, finding romance and new perspectives. Brooklynite Lohanny Santos posted a simple question in August asking if anyone in London wanted to trade apartments for two weeks, even using the film's iconic theme music in her video. Marie Draxler from Notting Hill answered, and after just two FaceTime calls, they booked flights and swapped keys. The women left out jewelry, beauty products, and personal touches that made it feel like staying with a friend rather than a stranger, and [their parallel adventures](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGSpCAUAL8GMyx5IJ4BwSVmc-mFFsJjZAfFgjda_v3W7C9s1lFXV_9_zA8bU9yf_-yGhkKed7xqQiIss1tLrLTZ6u1dqcQX1pKXTHWGDnougMMwexvTPavGm4AIKBs0YtD6j5S96prVjMsw2iWMziFopbzUvnfCLxACIIihieZjEwCDEF0vx9bJfkHthFa6t530THXoI-XSfuW404oh3fnE0rq8NcmsREdl8i-DicG0vEsGNbQbMphSDFjpTh-mYk9MDKaCmDhlXtAmfuUNd553TvFF-cVtQV4vexxxf_I1vytWHnLhp3WWYt4rg6kpxh9XTClWPTDVPTcb008M_ergaeA7AAD__5oRh_M) drew tens of thousands of followers who cheered them on daily. The swap has since sparked a movement, with women across the globe now pitching their own home exchanges from Seattle to Vienna.

clean_body: U.S. Africa Command confirmed Thursday that in northwest Nigeria after militants allegedly targeted and brutally killed primarily Christian civilians. | | | | | | Saturday, December 27, 2025 ---|--- Good morning! On this day in 1968, NASA’s splashed down in the Pacific, completing the first crewed mission to orbit the Moon. Two women recreated The Holiday by between Brooklyn and London after a single social media post, as we report below, turning a movie fantasy into a real-life transatlantic adventure that drew millions of viewers along for the ride. Today’s sponsor is . Winter hydration matters more than most people realize, especially in cold air, at high altitudes, and during long days outside. Whether you are skiing, traveling, or just staying active in colder weather, helps you stay properly hydrated without sugar or unnecessary additives. | US Strikes ISIS in Nigeria U.S. Africa Command confirmed Thursday that in northwest Nigeria after militants allegedly targeted and brutally killed primarily Christian civilians. The strikes followed President Trump's warning that "there would be hell to pay" if killings of Christians continued. Officials provided no details on casualties, targets, or the number of strikes. Nigerian officials said they are working with U.S. partners to fight terrorism and protect lives, and kidnappings in the north while denying systematic persecution of Christians. Nigeria's between Muslims in the north and Christians in the south, where a humanitarian group says more than 7,000 Christians were killed this year. Biggest Car Trends for 2026 Take Shape Automakers are lining up an unusually crowded 2026 model year, with nearly globally, and many aimed squarely at U.S. buyers. The common thread across hybrids, refreshed gas models, and carefully targeted EVs--rather than an all-in electric push. On the business side, the U.S. car market is finally , with inventories improving and prices cooling slightly, although affordability remains the biggest hurdle for buyers. Bottom line for 2026: more choices on dealer lots, fewer moonshots, and a sharper focus on what American drivers are actually willing--and able--to buy. Life Imitates Rom-Com Two women recreated the 2006 film The Holiday by , and millions watched it unfold on TikTok. In the Nancy Meyers movie, Cameron Diaz and Kate Winslet trade homes between Los Angeles and the English countryside, finding romance and new perspectives. Brooklynite Lohanny Santos posted a simple question in August asking if anyone in London wanted to trade apartments for two weeks, even using the film's iconic theme music in her video. Marie Draxler from Notting Hill answered, and after just two FaceTime calls, they booked flights and swapped keys. The women left out jewelry, beauty products, and personal touches that made it feel like staying with a friend rather than a stranger, and drew tens of thousands of followers who cheered them on daily. The swap has since sparked a movement, with women across the globe now pitching their own home exchanges from Seattle to Vienna. Next month, this opportunity will close--and I hope you'll consider joining us before it closes for good. But here's the deal: we only have four days left in 2025 , and I'm asking you to consider investing before year's end. Four days from now, the year ends. Next month, this investment round closes. And once it's closed, this opportunity is gone. If you've been watching from the sidelines, this is the moment to step in and help us finish 2025 strong--and position yourself alongside a media company that's growing while legacy media continues to shrink. 👉 You Have Questions. We Have Answers. One of the most common--and important--questions we receive is simple and fair: How do I get a return on my investment? We believe in being direct. All investments carry risk--but here's what makes The Flyover different: We became profitable after just two years, with strong margins and disciplined growth, placing us among the top tier of media startups in the country. There are three primary ways The Flyover investors could ultimately see returns: 1\. A Sale A larger media company or strategic partner could acquire The Flyover. If that day comes, we'll only partner with organizations that respect our mission and our readers. 2\. A Public Listing While we're private today, a future public listing could allow investors to buy and sell shares freely. 3\. Profit Distributions (Dividends) Unlike most startups, we're already generating profit. That makes future distributions a real possibility as the business continues to scale. To recap: 1\. Sale. 2. Listing. 3. Distributions. The Final Push of 2025 We're in a 4-day sprint to close out the year, with our funding round scheduled to close next month. Shares are limited, and once this round is filled, no additional investments will be accepted. If you've been considering investing--whether to support the mission of fact-based journalism, to own a piece of a growing media company, or to position yourself for potential returns--this is your moment. Will you help us finish 2025 strong? Join us as an owner before the year--and this round--comes to a close. 👉 P.S. disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado
body:
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001LO0ycAR2_enNGcKgBJPo3ZBfr_JYbpEDlxVs1qcYsAQDCMiOrOT4BxIDXb2X034OQ8zfhKfYsu7LeU3aFsz_Eis17ZBbdFvUc2M3JG4MvQUMxVuFT--PiE42GhzVF8kmPdpFjyNIWOEtCk2jt79EJTIAK_dHSqb-1DKRf8slIgyROAgp9HHIILVIzPcR5H3BAo-SmmaYAau-urpIN5H07wNJoyy6-dfx&c=rR0hBLMIlp_IF3fycPAuR_b8UMCj940lrHVXQHDDX4aBxAATuUT3AQ==&ch=xlSUiQCMmUOpa4X4KzXnwuSq51N_WmE_6L3zTF9cE0vRTsQXe2A0pQ==)
---
|

Hi Peter -- I'm Mike Nelson -- a longtime news meteorologist and proud Coloradan.
After decades of tracking tornadoes, wildfires, floods and blizzards across our state, I've seen firsthand how timely, reliable weather reporting saves lives - especially in rural areas where help can be hours away.
But recently, Lauren Boebert and her friends in Congress have slashed critical weather and emergency preparedness funding. She backed the "Big, Beautiful Bill," which guts the systems we rely on to predict and prepare for extreme weather. That's not just irresponsible. It's dangerous.
That's why I'm standing with Trisha Calvarese. She actually grew up here. She knows our communities, shows up and listens. And she understands how important it is to protect the resources families count on - weather alerts, a reliable electric grid, emergency services.
We need someone in Congress who puts public safety before partisan soundbites. Someone who takes facts seriously and doesn't gamble with people's lives. That's why I'm asking: [Will you chip in today to help Trisha flip this seat, hold Boebert accountable, and bring real leadership back to Colorado?](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001LO0ycAR2_enNGcKgBJPo3ZBfr_JYbpEDlxVs1qcYsAQDCMiOrOT4BxIDXb2X034OQ8zfhKfYsu7LeU3aFsz_Eis17ZBbdFvUc2M3JG4MvQUMxVuFT--PiE42GhzVF8kmPdpFjyNIWOEtCk2jt79EJTIAK_dHSqb-1DKRf8slIgyROAgp9HHIILVIzPcR5H3BAo-SmmaYAau-urpIN5H076ZIIuBLkNiC&c=rR0hBLMIlp_IF3fycPAuR_b8UMCj940lrHVXQHDDX4aBxAATuUT3AQ==&ch=xlSUiQCMmUOpa4X4KzXnwuSq51N_WmE_6L3zTF9cE0vRTsQXe2A0pQ==)

clean_body: | | | | | == | Hi Peter -- I'm Mike Nelson -- a longtime news meteorologist and proud Coloradan. After decades of tracking tornadoes, wildfires, floods and blizzards across our state, I've seen firsthand how timely, reliable weather reporting saves lives - especially in rural areas where help can be hours away. But recently, Lauren Boebert and her friends in Congress have slashed critical weather and emergency preparedness funding. She backed the "Big, Beautiful Bill," which guts the systems we rely on to predict and prepare for extreme weather. That's not just irresponsible. It's dangerous. That's why I'm standing with Trisha Calvarese. She actually grew up here. She knows our communities, shows up and listens. And she understands how important it is to protect the resources families count on - weather alerts, a reliable electric grid, emergency services. We need someone in Congress who puts public safety before partisan soundbites. Someone who takes facts seriously and doesn't gamble with people's lives. That's why I'm asking: == If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | == | == | == | | == | == | == | == Thanks for taking this seriously - because I sure do. \- Mike Nelson | | == | | | | == | Mail checks to: Trisha 4 CO 9609 S University Blvd., PO Box 631322 Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado | Trisha Calvarese for Congress | 9609 S University Blvd. PO Box 631322 | Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 US | |

**Good Morning!** On this day in 1944, [Eli Whiteley](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0D-O3SAQgPHT4G4tGPyPgiKNu02bEmGYZ0bCxoLxet_to-QKe4FPn35_Pt2np0zn_tsfaPn7VdyJT8vIjNUl3FpIlRofvu30YiPlQRlfDiSMHwo-YHbRU367oYt2k9GoDq2ap2nR02LmLtkwIYybQT0tWmu_LMp7KUc0EbZBj6Yj-y-mAGYFUmndb0aPZprjOIdhCTKIQWIkLrXnhIzfvr3yu3xh7UM5umwT89WE_iVgFbA-z9P7erz7g7KA1VemkFHACgMoOQhYI7aLGN1T7jM2F8pd_Y7lbq6VHAmryxhdouau7LmU03Gq5d6T2zxzxq7aeD2UM7VpFoPcD0_5_wv_TPDLwt8AAAD__1Q6hzc), who grew up on his family's farm near Georgetown, single-handedly killed nine German soldiers and captured 23 while leading his infantry platoon during house-to-house combat near Sigolsheim, France. Forcibly evacuated after losing an eye and being severely wounded in his arm and shoulder, he earned the Medal of Honor for his bravery. This is an abbreviated holiday edition of _The Texas Flyover_ , which allows our staff to spend time with family and friends. We'll be back with a full edition on Monday. Today’s sponsor, [Prime Biome](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDuKwzAUAE8jdzH62FJUqAgEd9l2S6PPk_1Aio31Np_bL9krbD_MwHzf5pvHgvfly1dw9MrbfIdnK0AEx7xCaHE9sFH1bcFMlvOKBfIsuRxPQp6kmZPH8p6HLrnAkxUdOGG0Pit9tqZbXRq9FlkOPkAO1ozRDl6PyooQUvQid-g-MiGlEZILpfpg1Wi1SaOJwznyyAYOCWk7elqB4OVbLu_tAUcft9oVtxLtjakLkxOT0wK0H1gh4FbhQzA5PTDB1u_rztTkc8aCnoCpa9hK8ju8BJOaMDF1zaX-NEqQpLlClOOlO1zan1gKNm3YwJfqsfyF6X-7Hk7-BgAA___DpX60), is sharing a simple probiotic skincare approach that’s helping people visibly reduce dark spots, eye bags, and fine lines—using just a few drops before bed.
* * *

But here's the deal: we only have **four days left in 2025** , and I'm asking you to consider investing before year's end. Four days from now, the year ends.
Next month, this investment round closes.
And once it's closed, this opportunity is gone. If you've been watching from the sidelines, this is the moment to step in and help us finish 2025 strong -- and position yourself alongside a media company that's growing while legacy media continues to shrink. [👉 **Invest in _The Flyover_ today** ](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2P2zAMhn-NvCWQJduyBg0FUm_pLQe0myBLtM1CH4HFJHf_vnCbAt27vg_5gHy_X-3VYcS8fnMJDH0sxWZ41ghEsNsN5uq3HSslV1dcSHOeMMJiBRf9qRUnoWxwGD9t1wQz86DbBkyrhmGUw6hVs5nRz1xwp7t-VnoYWi07LUPLFz_6sZOyQXPIWiFUK3gr5XnWsteDCr3y3ei5Zx2HgFT2M21A8OHqEj_LA_azL6mJZiO6VSa_MDExMWF-QKXzz4KZNvhn8sByulOytdx3D0xevgYkLJmJ4YgTBLwnJi9vzwzhdIWA7oW8SzeHa2byMv0x_gUlE2Q6lt6nV0awH5b3H9ObPd4SfbObcHtijFgHxTq-Jofx9_X0f50_jPgVAAD__5wAlT4)

**You Have Questions. We Have Answers.** One of the most common -- and important -- questions we receive is simple and fair: **How do I get a return on my investment?** We believe in being direct. All investments carry risk -- but here's what makes _The Flyover_ different: We became profitable after just two years, with strong margins and disciplined growth, placing us among the top tier of media startups in the country. There are three primary ways _Flyover_ investors could ultimately see returns: **1\. A Sale**
A larger media company or strategic partner could acquire _The Flyover_. If that day comes, we'll only partner with organizations that respect our mission and our readers. **2\. A Public Listing**
While we're private today, a future public listing could allow investors to buy and sell shares freely. **3\. Profit Distributions (Dividends)**
Unlike most startups, we're already generating profit. That makes future distributions a real possibility as the business continues to scale. To recap:
**1\. Sale. 2. Listing. 3. Distributions.**
* * *
**The Final Push of 2025** We're in a **4-day sprint** to close out the year, with our funding round scheduled to close next month. Shares are limited, and once this round is filled, no additional investments will be accepted. If you've been considering investing -- whether to support the mission of fact-based journalism, to own a piece of a growing media company, or to position yourself for potential returns -- **this is your moment**. ## App Store Age-Verification Law Blocked
A federal judge in Austin temporarily [blocked the implementation](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGSrCAURVeDGRY8VCQg-IlZ_3RCC-GhzGBjAd12737K3sJs4Nxzz9dtvpkQw339b3bU9eXTfMezRKwV87zhUuyWQ6m7KWvwVTG2h4h-BgY95UBBzs6E-J67xumFOcUb1FwOwyiGUclm01yxnhmloEfBDO9GhlYJzwTjcpC-a4K-YBxAcmBciHZRoleDdL203WiZJR1DF2rKbd2w4ssUH9_pibm1aW-i3mo9ChH_CEwEpvM82-_H5dymvBKYrj8EpmuEwMSBwPQo1KPDbCK16ZErXWKyP4V-4NQcBy01ZaRmRfrEHHywpoZ0p9GcBKYma3ecIcZQBkk6tu4mxI9M_VvCp4bfAAAA__-1tYP5) of a new state law that requires app stores to ensure children under the age of 18 obtain parental consent before downloading apps or making purchases. Senate Bill 2420, known as the App Store Accountability Act, was scheduled to take effect on Jan. 1. The Computer & Communications Industry Association [challenged the constitutionality](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL-SqyAUh59GOxxEBSkobmOX227pIBz07KI4cBKTt99JXmH735_5vq_bfLMY8Vj_2x0MPUOaD7hKBCLI8wZLcVvGQrstKwbSnO8YIcyCi4G1ggk1e4vxNfe1Nwv3uq3BtErKsZOjVvVm5KD7IXA_jKBtO2rZuZHzfgzLYqWzvEbzHmuFUK3gbdc1i-4GLZUflOtHx13Vc_BIKTe0AcHTlhBf6QG5cWmvo9mIzlJ1_yoxVWK6rqsJ6ansvRAe70glpjdTJaZPmUV7MbsCy1AooyM8VmbPkxVKGQpbYnI_4FkAD9lG9n33K9TZ-PPCGLFIVfV83S3Gzz_9zdrDiN8AAAD__z6FgnQ) of the law on behalf of tech giants such as Apple, Google, and Amazon, arguing that the law violates the First Amendment right to free speech. Two Texas teens and a youth-led advocacy group also filed a lawsuit to block the statute. "_The Act is akin to a law that would require every bookstore to verify the age of every customer at the door and, for minors, require parental consent before the child or teen could enter and again when they try to purchase a book_ ," said Obama-appointed U.S. District Judge Robert Pitman in his [order](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2SozAUhE8jMrmkJ0AoULAJ2Wy6IfXQD2gsEIXeGPv2W_YVJu2u7ur-_n1NX5hy2pe_uAVLz1imPVw1B6JwTmuYq1vPVGnDuqRIRogt5RAnENBxCRz05DHl19Q23s7CG9kEK3XfD6ofjG5Wq0w_mNYo1wqUAvUsvIQYjYoz-g6GJtl3mQTQEoRU6jYb1Zle-067dnDCsVYEn6icN1oDhSfWmF_lEc6bK1uT7Up0VKb-MBgZjNd13e5P3N8mg_H9hsH4iTEYv3_8Evici7tX_hE5HgevVM7A0bnysxPOKSd6cXTE41k2TnhP-8JDjMER_8adSwZjc1p_XCnnVHvNWrFsmPJnEv0O5MPC_wAAAP__uDqITA). State Sen. Angela Paxton (R-McKinney), the bill's author, expressed confidence that Pitman's order would be overturned. "_We built this bill to equip parents with common sense tools to protect their kids AND to survive court challenges by those who may have lesser priorities_ ," [she said](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGy4yAMRb8Gd2RAOAYXFNu4e9tu6cEgbDY4eED7nPz9jvMLr9OM5hzp3j9f85dLOT3X325HS69Y5ieeLSMR1nnDpfmtpka7a2uKNAqxp4xxBgF3LoGDnoNL-T33XbCLCKPs0Eo9DEYNZtTdZiU4MwpljHPK6zGKYECpcDe9jFIs2CV7ySSAliCkUrdlVPdx0OGufW-88KwXGBKVeqMNCV-uxfwu31hvvuxdthvR0Zj6xWBiMJ3neXs0R9eSwXSlYTB9MAbTdYrBJOGaFYMpYsDqMv_7L6zICfejVFdTfvMlF_9o_EPy7E5esVFNntJz5Y8UGo-17NwdB29UKl76rtpwnCnn1AbNerHuLuXPm_Szcr8t_A8AAP__9bqNiA) in a statement.
## H-E-B Gifts Free Groceries After Tech Glitch
Shoppers at the H-E-B in Burleson got an early Christmas surprise on Monday when the store [gave away free groceries](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDuO7CAQRVdjZ7Sg_MEOCF7SWb90QgtDYUrCDYKa_ux-1LOFSe-Vjo7O1227WUp0P_7bEw2_Qt7u-GwJmbFuEffmYqXGp20HBV6lPClh2EDCJBQI0Ju3lN7b2HuzS7-qHo3S87wM87LqPpqwar3s6BYAqycI0o_j7JWSNjgHg-_JfGAKQCuQahgu-zpM66z9pN24OOm6UaInzvXCERlftoX0zg-sF5fPPpnIXFo3_Ovg2sG1YC4JP1cH1yhQ7OKgBzbRYi4FaxOhIoqjZoeVsAkbGKuIOZG3b-HyWb4_w5GIXRRKLXpateyr8eVJKVGbdTfK47SUfgX4b9keBn4CAAD__39ggaY) after its computers went down. As checkout lines began to back up, a manager named Destanie [made an announcement](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD1uhDAUhE8DnZH_jQsXaeg2bUpkzDM8yaxXtrPs3j4iUk6QbqQZzeibr9t885jwvn36A1x7xTzf4awJWoMy77DUsBes7fB1w9gspQcmiDOnXBHGCTfz6jG9Z9mvbqGrZT04ZrQehR6t6XcngDETtRAClGZKgZKjWrTSi_USrOrRXWWMc8M4ZUIMixXKarMqE-QYaOgkhRVbLkPbocHL15je-QllCPnok9tbe9ROfHR86vh0nucQ80teGFeg49MlOz7tBMhCNnxCJeG7tnxAqSQWALKVHKDgZfzh9sWtjxNTwqpNJ-l2eEy_i-1_Pz0d_wkAAP__bhB8bw) that was initially met with groans but quickly led to cheers. "_Unfortunately, the computers are not coming back up right now. Today, everything that you have, we are going to bag you up. And we hope every one of you have a very Merry Christmas and continues to shop with us_ ,” she said. Arriving guests were turned away, while those in line were allowed to leave with their groceries without paying. One shopper [said](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0D2OpDAUBODTmOy1bAN2ExCQkM0mq9WGyD8PbMnGyH47TN9-1XOFyUoq6VOp_n5sHyameB6_TMaZvvaynXi3hERYt4C2uVBjo2zaEXeaOM8x4b5JLkcQEqTevInptQ2dny33k-hwFlqpZ6-ek-7CbPfROi-V5m7UXomBT1bY5zjywShn-y7Ob0xIqYXkou8fdurHSWk_ajc8HXds4OgjlfqggIRfpu3pVT6xPlzJXZoD0dVYvzC5Mrne9_3I7Xx3TK54wr_G5NquUukdCgWsTK5nqRTgW4MWynVhbXAUCCUj3JEC7BURAiBYOGpxWCM2MDthhfZqhBmOFMkFJldTYVnE7z9Lr7o6--uOKcWmNBv4kU1M30PpZ_d-zvJ_AAAA__-VHo9l) the gesture "brought tears to my eyes," and another said he "couldn't believe it." "_H-E-B is committed to serving our customers year-round and especially during the holidays_ ," a company spokesperson said in a statement. “ _The customer experience is at the heart of what we do!_ "

clean_body: Shoppers at the H-E-B in Burleson got an early Christmas surprise on Monday when the store after its computers went down. | | | | | | Saturday, December 27, 2025 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1944, , who grew up on his family's farm near Georgetown, single-handedly killed nine German soldiers and captured 23 while leading his infantry platoon during house-to-house combat near Sigolsheim, France. Forcibly evacuated after losing an eye and being severely wounded in his arm and shoulder, he earned the Medal of Honor for his bravery. This is an abbreviated holiday edition of The Texas Flyover , which allows our staff to spend time with family and friends. We'll be back with a full edition on Monday. Today’s sponsor, , is sharing a simple probiotic skincare approach that’s helping people visibly reduce dark spots, eye bags, and fine lines—using just a few drops before bed. Take** The Flyoverwith you. Busy day? Listen to our daily 15-minute podcast while commuting, cooking, or walking the dog—your Flyover fix, anywhere. | | The Biggest Question of 2025...And 4 Days Left to Answer It Just a few months ago, we set out on a mission: to invite a small, exclusive group of Flyover readers to become Flyover owners. Next month, this opportunity will close -- and I hope you'll consider joining us before it closes for good. But here's the deal: we only have four days left in 2025 , and I'm asking you to consider investing before year's end. Four days from now, the year ends. Next month, this investment round closes. And once it's closed, this opportunity is gone. If you've been watching from the sidelines, this is the moment to step in and help us finish 2025 strong -- and position yourself alongside a media company that's growing while legacy media continues to shrink. You Have Questions. We Have Answers. One of the most common -- and important -- questions we receive is simple and fair: How do I get a return on my investment? We believe in being direct. All investments carry risk -- but here's what makes The Flyover different: We became profitable after just two years, with strong margins and disciplined growth, placing us among the top tier of media startups in the country. There are three primary ways Flyover investors could ultimately see returns: 1\. A Sale A larger media company or strategic partner could acquire The Flyover. If that day comes, we'll only partner with organizations that respect our mission and our readers. 2\. A Public Listing While we're private today, a future public listing could allow investors to buy and sell shares freely. 3\. Profit Distributions (Dividends) Unlike most startups, we're already generating profit. That makes future distributions a real possibility as the business continues to scale. To recap: 1\. Sale. 2. Listing. 3. Distributions. The Final Push of 2025 We're in a 4-day sprint to close out the year, with our funding round scheduled to close next month. Shares are limited, and once this round is filled, no additional investments will be accepted. If you've been considering investing -- whether to support the mission of fact-based journalism, to own a piece of a growing media company, or to position yourself for potential returns -- this is your moment. Will you help us finish 2025 strong? Join us as an owner before the year -- and this round -- comes to a close. P.S. A 15% bonus share opportunity is still available during this end-of-year sprint -- and once it's gone, it's gone. | App Store Age-Verification Law Blocked A federal judge in Austin temporarily of a new state law that requires app stores to ensure children under the age of 18 obtain parental consent before downloading apps or making purchases. Senate Bill 2420, known as the App Store Accountability Act, was scheduled to take effect on Jan. 1. The Computer & Communications Industry Association of the law on behalf of tech giants such as Apple, Google, and Amazon, arguing that the law violates the First Amendment right to free speech. Two Texas teens and a youth-led advocacy group also filed a lawsuit to block the statute. "The Act is akin to a law that would require every bookstore to verify the age of every customer at the door and, for minors, require parental consent before the child or teen could enter and again when they try to purchase a book ," said Obama-appointed U.S. District Judge Robert Pitman in his . State Sen. Angela Paxton (R-McKinney), the bill's author, expressed confidence that Pitman's order would be overturned. "We built this bill to equip parents with common sense tools to protect their kids AND to survive court challenges by those who may have lesser priorities ," in a statement. H-E-B Gifts Free Groceries After Tech Glitch Shoppers at the H-E-B in Burleson got an early Christmas surprise on Monday when the store after its computers went down. As checkout lines began to back up, a manager named Destanie that was initially met with groans but quickly led to cheers. "Unfortunately, the computers are not coming back up right now. Today, everything that you have, we are going to bag you up. And we hope every one of you have a very Merry Christmas and continues to shop with us ,” she said. Arriving guests were turned away, while those in line were allowed to leave with their groceries without paying. One shopper the gesture "brought tears to my eyes," and another said he "couldn't believe it." "H-E-B is committed to serving our customers year-round and especially during the holidays ," a company spokesperson said in a statement. “ The customer experience is at the heart of what we do! Trump announced late Tuesday that the United States launched a “powerful and deadly” military strike against Islamic State terrorists operating in northwest Nigeria, citing brutal attacks against Christians in the region. | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. Dear USO Supporter,
This may not be something you hear about, but it should be making headlines: **2025 has been a tough year for our military and their families.**
With threats continuing to emerge around the globe, natural disasters and other challenges, our service members are being called to protect our nation -- and this isn't going to slow down.
**What we raise in the next five days can impact how much we 're able to support our service members in 2026. [Now is YOUR chance to have your tax-deductible gift MATCHED for our military heroes, wherever they'll be called to serve in the new year.](https://links.e.uso.)**
**For the next 48 hours, your gift will be MATCHED by our friends at Leonardo DRS, up to a total of $35,000 to help us reach our critical year-end goal to support service members.**

**[$100 DOUBLES TO $200](https://links.e.uso.)**

This holiday season, more than 200,000 troops are standing guard all over the world, missing family celebrations while they protect our freedoms. Given the challenges our heroes have faced this year, **they 're in critical need of your support.**
**[Please, will you rush a tax-deductible donation now while your gift will DOUBLE?](https://links.e.uso.)** Your generous gift can help provide more care packages, hand-made meals, Wi-Fi for our heroes to call home and so much more.

name: Team Trisha
subject: Blood in the water
body:
Our shocking victory in Boebert’s most important county, showed that Democrats, independents, and Republicans alike are fed up with MAGA and Boebert.
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Ywguy3hX303_eetG-ZN6P4aBKdm0unj0NCmE7k-U7drr4FC_rTIDpMEBYYTr-TiR9KjARakogjzumNhuEq4vbF8tk7VSHE-A16RrUV5wl82tGhxAB5TlMVevWHB5wbquRc8hOAu8HBp2oFislqbpU6zjQNfN85Ji-Mhnnt7RkiuhSgOux7WCdWFiKkYER_lbqXFXKXLYnQEeVp69AscvF8BMP4tnKUPeFerL0Il1-XQ=&c=ABXOMUP7P7j-XZFSXTWQB9VHVu7M1lX90zXmD3Z5nR-Tw0_8f-rRwA==&ch=YNwYJanoO4YWfUNYT0ZuRllyi8Kwpuj5GqoAqhqQd1gyxFw6-ry_hg==)
---
---
| Peter,
To say that Lauren Boebert is vulnerable in the upcoming 2026 midterms is an understatement.
After reading this we hope you will understand why we so wholeheartedly believe that Trisha Cavarese can beat Boebert in 2026.
In June 2025, MAGA launched a lightning push to pass a "home rule" ballot initiative in Douglas County, the most populous county in Lauren Boebert's congressional district. Home rule would have allowed this red county to create its own constitution and in a sense secede from the state putting the county at odds with the Governor and legislature.
Against all odds, Democrats managed the effort to stop home rule in Douglas County and together we defeated the initiative with a landslide 70% of the vote.
---
| [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Ywguy3hX303_eetG-ZN6P4aBKdm0unj0NCmE7k-U7drr4FC_rTIDpMEBYYTr-TiR9KjARakogjzumNhuEq4vbF8tk7VSHE-A16RrUV5wl82tGhxAB5TlMVevWHB5wbquRc8hOAu8HBp2oFislqbpU6zjQNfN85Ji-Mhnnt7RkiuhSgOux7WCdWFiKkYER_lbqXFXKXLYnQEeVp69AscvF8BMP4tnKUPebKgFTKs_dUs=&c=ABXOMUP7P7j-XZFSXTWQB9VHVu7M1lX90zXmD3Z5nR-Tw0_8f-rRwA==&ch=YNwYJanoO4YWfUNYT0ZuRllyi8Kwpuj5GqoAqhqQd1gyxFw6-ry_hg==)
---
Our shocking victory in Boebert's most important county, showed that Democrats, independents, and Republicans alike are fed up with MAGA and Boebert.
Boebert is only in Congress because of 546 votes. In 2024, when Trisha faced Boebert, our campaign had the best performance any Democrat has had in this district ever and outperformed Kamala Harris by 6 points.
This combined with the deep unpopularity of the GOP and Trump, the fact that Boebert is seen as a carpetbagger (she moved hundreds of miles to CD4 after it became clear she would lose in CD3), and the Boebert campaign reporting their lowest ever fundraising this year, means that she is critically vulnerable for 2026.
There is blood in the water and everyone will soon know that Boebert is a paper tiger. She can be beaten this election. [Can we count on you to make a donation of $25 or whatever you can spare to get Trisha the firepower that she will need to demolish Boebert in this coming election?](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Ywguy3hX303_eetG-ZN6P4aBKdm0unj0NCmE7k-U7drr4FC_rTIDpMEBYYTr-TiRO94mZ419hmad-JNMb-qV0Ghq96zPcazDU_IZADRgMAgXFmTaboHPqQ3CHIhZ6XFoLq4TIopMnL3wpnBJ9-vaQelDAwso4Q-W4dCkYQF0NOKY87AjHtmmSevneUz6zXDZI-arvD1JwMlQADPQ5U3JQcnvAi2bC_dtM9xmrK-Jf4PhwyKrgHZSUcMkBKo7qbe4&c=ABXOMUP7P7j-XZFSXTWQB9VHVu7M1lX90zXmD3Z5nR-Tw0_8f-rRwA==&ch=YNwYJanoO4YWfUNYT0ZuRllyi8Kwpuj5GqoAqhqQd1gyxFw6-ry_hg==)

clean_body: Our shocking victory in Boebert’s most important county, showed that Democrats, independents, and Republicans alike are fed up with MAGA and Boebert. | | | | | == | Peter, To say that Lauren Boebert is vulnerable in the upcoming 2026 midterms is an understatement. After reading this we hope you will understand why we so wholeheartedly believe that Trisha Cavarese can beat Boebert in 2026. In June 2025, MAGA launched a lightning push to pass a "home rule" ballot initiative in Douglas County, the most populous county in Lauren Boebert's congressional district. Home rule would have allowed this red county to create its own constitution and in a sense secede from the state putting the county at odds with the Governor and legislature. Against all odds, Democrats managed the effort to stop home rule in Douglas County and together we defeated the initiative with a landslide 70% of the vote. | == Our shocking victory in Boebert's most important county, showed that Democrats, independents, and Republicans alike are fed up with MAGA and Boebert. Boebert is only in Congress because of 546 votes. In 2024, when Trisha faced Boebert, our campaign had the best performance any Democrat has had in this district ever and outperformed Kamala Harris by 6 points. This combined with the deep unpopularity of the GOP and Trump, the fact that Boebert is seen as a carpetbagger (she moved hundreds of miles to CD4 after it became clear she would lose in CD3), and the Boebert campaign reporting their lowest ever fundraising this year, means that she is critically vulnerable for 2026. There is blood in the water and everyone will soon know that Boebert is a paper tiger. She can be beaten this election. == If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | == | == | == | | == | == | == | == Thank you for being a part of this team. Trisha Calvarese | | | | == | Mail checks to: Trisha 4 CO 9609 S University Blvd., PO Box 631322 Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 | Trisha Calvarese for Congress | 9609 S University Blvd. PO Box 631322 | Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 US | |

name: Douglas Carswell
subject: A Look Back at an Extraordinary 2025 – Thank You!
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/mspolicy/alookback?e=5703353baa)
---
| |
---
| _**If you're not in front of our Saturday email, you're behind.**_
---
| Dear Friend,
**98.7% of 2025 is now over – and what a wonderful year it has been for the Mississippi Center for Public Policy! **

Thanks to your support, we’ve made meaningful, lasting progress in advancing liberty and sound policy across our state. **Here are just a few of the highlights you helped make possible:**

1. **Historic income tax reform**
Together, we helped secure landmark income tax reform- an achievement so significant that MCPP was proudly co-awarded the prestigious Bob Williams Award by our peers in the State Policy Network.

Here are some moments from that well-earned recognition:
---
|
---
|
1. **Direct impact with state leaders**
We hosted Mississippi’s key policymakers at several high-energy events attended by hundreds of engaged citizens from every corner of the state.

A few scenes from those powerful gatherings:
---
|
---
|
1. **Investing in the next generation**
A record number of bright young Mississippians graduated from our Mississippi Leadership Academy this year, gaining a deeper understanding of the moral case for free markets and the timeless principles that built America.

Celebrating the future leaders we’re helping shape:
---
|
---
|
1. **Thought leadership across Mississippi and beyond**
More than 50 of our op-eds appeared a remarkable 133 times in newspapers throughout the state \- and even reached audiences across America.

Our message in print, making an impact:
---
|
---
|
1. **Strong digital reach – 100% organic**
Over 83,000 people watched an average of more than 10 minutes of our studio content, and we generated millions of impressions across social media - all through genuine engagement (no bots, no paid views, just real results!).

---
|
---
| Our small but highly productive team is so grateful that we have partners like you who make this work possible.

I’m already looking forward to sharing exciting updates in the new year about the key reforms we plan to advance in 2026.

Wishing you and your loved ones a joyful New Year celebration. I hope to see you soon in 2026!
---
| [Forward this email to a friend!](https://mspolicy.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=f85ce08988&e=5703353baa "Forward this email to a friend!")
---
| |
---
Warm regards,

Douglas Carswell
President & CEO
---
| P.S. A huge thank you again — your support truly makes the difference.
---
|
---
| | _ _
You are receiving this email because you opted in via our website.

**Our mailing address is:**
Mississippi Center for Public Policy520 George StJackson, MS 39202-3013
[Add us to your address book](https://mspolicy.us18.list-manage.com/vcard?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=2f68021ebd)

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](https://mcpp-c58f.mailchimpsites.com/manage/preferences?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=2f68021ebd&e=5703353baa&c=1d54b99f69) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://mspolicy.us18.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=2f68021ebd&t=b&e=5703353baa&c=1d54b99f69).

_Copyright © 2025 Mississippi Center for Public Policy, All rights reserved._
---

name: JB Pritzker
subject: re: Georgia
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jon Ossoff for SenateThis email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail
body:
#

[
---
Peter, it’s JB Pritzker. In a minute, I’m going to ask you to [**join me in supporting Jon Ossoff by chipping in to his reelection campaign.**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.lXrKfY&refcode=em251227-1-full&t=2&akid=1325%2E5691347%2EbMXam_) But first, I hope you’ll let me explain why Georgia’s Senate race will be one of the most important elections on the map in 2026: _We aren’t even a year into Trump’s second term, and he’s already forcing his unpopular and harmful agenda on the American people._ He’s stripping folks of their health care, cutting veterans’ services, and threatening to revoke citizenship from American citizens — all while he makes unprecedented grabs for power and attempts to establish himself as an authoritarian strongman. Look, it’s clear that as Trump’s madman tendencies grip the nation and harm working families, we need leaders everywhere to step up and fight back to protect our democracy. **Jon Ossoff has proven himself to be one of those leaders, Peter.** Before being elected by the people of Georgia, Jon led a team of investigative journalists that uncovered human rights abuses and unjust practices by corrupt governments. He’s brought that knowledge to the Senate where he’s acted as a watchdog for the Trump administration’s unconstitutional overreach on our government. We cannot lose Jon Ossoff’s leadership in the Senate. But Republicans have made him their top target in 2026, meaning this will likely be the most competitive and expensive election in the country. Jon can defend his seat and continue his important work in Washington — but he needs folks to step up and help him win this race. [**Peter, will you join me in supporting Jon Ossoff’s reelection campaign by pitching in any amount today? Whether it’s $5, $10, or whatever you’re able to give, it’ll go a long way in a race this important.**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.lXrKfY&refcode=em251227-1-full&t=3&akid=1325%2E5691347%2EbMXam_) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_ | | **[CONTRIBUTE $5](https://act.electjon.com/go/1686?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.zuSSTi&refcode=em251227-1-full&t=22&akid=1325%2E5691347%2EbMXam_)**
---
**** | **[CONTRIBUTE $25](https://act.electjon.com/go/1526?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.3llSsh&refcode=em251227-1-full&t=23&akid=1325%2E5691347%2EbMXam_)**
---
****
| | **[CONTRIBUTE $50](https://act.electjon.com/go/1520?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.bGqFYH&refcode=em251227-1-full&t=24&akid=1325%2E5691347%2EbMXam_)**
---
**** | **[CONTRIBUTE $100](https://act.electjon.com/go/1521?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.IZ7DFn&refcode=em251227-1-full&t=25&akid=1325%2E5691347%2EbMXam_)**
---
****
| | **[CONTRIBUTE $250](https://act.electjon.com/go/1522?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.pWvP31&refcode=em251227-1-full&t=26&akid=1325%2E5691347%2EbMXam_)**
---
**** | **[Another Amount](https://act.electjon.com/go/34?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.PyZHDd&refcode=em251227-1-full&t=27&akid=1325%2E5691347%2EbMXam_)**

****
Thank you in advance for standing with Jon. \- JB Pritzker

name: Angela Alsobrooks
subject: less-known, but no less important
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alsobrooks for Senate
body:
|
---
[ ](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/2092?t=1&akid=3421%2E52803%2EiJMnes)
| | Much of the work I've done this year involved issues that don’t make front-page headlines, Peter. They’re issues that affect us quietly; **lesser-known, but no less important.** **I’d like to tell you about a few things I’ve been working on in 2025 and will continue in 2026,**[**but before I do,****will you take a moment to chip in just $5, $10, whatever you can, to help me deliver for the hardworking people of Maryland?**](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/39265?t=10&amount=10&akid=3421%2E52803%2EiJMnes) | | | [Chip in](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/39265?t=11&amount=10&akid=3421%2E52803%2EiJMnes)
---
* **Investing in women’s health:** I introduced the _Healthy Maternal and Obstetric Medicine (MOM) Act_ because currently, health insurance companies don’t consider pregnancy to be a qualifying event for a special enrollment period. The _Healthy MOM Act_ would change that.

* **Protecting federal workers during future shutdowns:** I introduced and co-sponsored several bills to freeze student loan and mortgage payments, grant access to unemployment benefits and retirement funds, and guarantee back pay for all federal workers.

* **Defending civil rights and liberties:** The _Voter Purge Protection Act_ would halt GOP voter purges and require officials to notify voters before removing them from the rolls; the _Restoring Access to Detainees Act_ would force ICE to allow people in custody regular contact with their lawyers; and the _Resolution Against Book Banning_ would address the growing problem of book banning, which is an attack on Americans' free speech.

* **Keeping communities safe** with the _No Nuclear Testing Act,_ which would ban use of federal funds for explosive nuclear tests, and the _Background Check Expansion Act,_ which would require background checks for nearly all gun sales.

That’s not all. I’m also working to pass legislation to keep Social Security benefits aligned with inflation, stop Trump’s taxpayer-funded Argentina bailout, protect SNAP and Head Start programs, and more.
**I have a long to-do list in 2026.** Your grassroots support helps me update you regularly on my efforts both on and off the Hill, especially on issues that don’t get the coverage they deserve.
**[If you can, will you pitch in $10 to help me continue removing barriers to success, tackling long-standing injustices, and building a BOLD and BRIGHT future for Maryland?](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/39265?t=12&amount=10&akid=3421%2E52803%2EiJMnes)**
| | [Chip in $5](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/39265?t=13&amount=5&akid=3421%2E52803%2EiJMnes)
---
| | [Chip in $10](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/39265?t=14&amount=10&akid=3421%2E52803%2EiJMnes)
---
| | [Chip in $25](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/39265?t=15&amount=25&akid=3421%2E52803%2EiJMnes)
---
| | [Chip in $50](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/39265?t=16&amount=50&akid=3421%2E52803%2EiJMnes)
---
| | [Chip in $100](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/39265?t=17&amount=100&akid=3421%2E52803%2EiJMnes)
---
| | [Custom Amount](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/39265?t=9&akid=3421%2E52803%2EiJMnes)
---
Together,
Angela Alsobrooks
| | [ ](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/89?t=2&akid=3421%2E52803%2EiJMnes)
---
| | An important update from Team Alsobrooks:

Angela is so thankful for this grassroots team’s support and is eager to get to work for Marylanders in the Senate. That’s why you’ll be receiving fewer fundraising emails from us moving forward, in an effort to not clog up your inbox for the next six years.

Instead, we’d rather send you emails you want to read – like updates on Angela’s work in the Senate, or rundowns of key issues and news stories from Washington.

But to make this possible, we’re relying on supporters like you to consider making a small, monthly recurring contribution of $3 or even $5 to keep our operations running while Angela is busy fighting for Maryland families.

[Make a recurring contribution to Angela Alsobrooks' campaign here.](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/33703?t=3&akid=3421%2E52803%2EiJMnes)
---
We believe that emails are the best way for our organization to stay in touch with supporters like you. But if you'd like to receive fewer emails, [click here.](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/signup/fewer-emails/?t=4&akid=3421%2E52803%2EiJMnes)
| | [ Chip in a one-time donation ](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/4693?t=5&akid=3421%2E52803%2EiJMnes)
---
| | [ Chip in a monthly recurring donation! ](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/4694?t=6&akid=3421%2E52803%2EiJMnes)
---
| If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please click [here to unsubscribe](https://alsobrooks.actionkit.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=7&akid=3421%2E52803%2EiJMnes).
| Paid for by Alsobrooks for Senate

Largo, MD 20792

A look back at 2025 | | [View in browser](https://xdzgmz.clicks.mlsend.com/ty/cl/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEyMDEyNjAsXCJsXCI6MTc0OTQ4NjI2MTA1MTA3ODM4LFwiclwiOjE3NDk0ODY2MDk5NzUyMzMwN30iLCJzIjoiMmUzZjJlYmJkNzcyYzJkYSJ9)

---
|
---
Peter, I hope you had a wonderful Christmas with the people you love. Before the year ends, I wanted to share something meaningful with you. This short **Year-in-Review video** captures what your support helped make possible in 2025 — and why the fight for medical freedom is just getting started.

|
---
| [ ](https://xdzgmz.clicks.mlsend.com/ty/cl/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEyMDEyNjAsXCJsXCI6MTc0OTQ4NjI2MTE2NjQyMTc3LFwiclwiOjE3NDk0ODY2MDk5NzUyMzMwN30iLCJzIjoiNjIwODFjZWQ4NGE4MzNlMyJ9)
---
|
---
This year, TFVC celebrated **10 years of impact** , welcomed the **MAHA movement to the White House** , won **Best of Festival** at the Santa Monica Film Festival for our documentary _Follow the Silenced_ , kicked off **Aaron Siri’s Texas book tour** , and helped pass **seven major medical liberty bills** while defeating every bad bill. Those wins happened because supporters like you stood in the gap. Now, as the year comes to a close, I’m asking you to help us carry this momentum into the next chapter. **Will you make a year-end gift to fuel the fights ahead?**

| | | [ ****Give Your Year-End Gift Today!**** ](https://xdzgmz.clicks.mlsend.com/ty/cl/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEyMDEyNjAsXCJsXCI6MTc0OTQ4NjI2MTIwODM2NDgzLFwiclwiOjE3NDk0ODY2MDk5NzUyMzMwN30iLCJzIjoiMDgwZjRjNDAzODA1ZjAzZiJ9)
---
|
---
Thank you for standing for freedom. Let’s finish this year strong.

|
---
| | | **Gratefully,
Rebecca Hardy
President, TFVC**

---|---|---
|
---
P.S. If you are looking to make a tax-deductible gift, consider the Texans for Vaccine Choice Education Foundation—the 501(c)(3) arm of TFVC—for your end-of-year generosity!

We’ve seen just how powerful our movement can be when we stand together to each do our part — and 2025 has been full of examples of ordinary people rising up:
* The volunteers in Missouri who gathered hundreds of thousands of petition signatures to put their gerrymandered map on the ballot for rejection.

* The Indiana voters who made clear to lawmakers they were against their attempted mid-decade map redraw.

* The millions of voters in Virginia, Pennsylvania, and beyond who voted for pro-democracy candidates up and down the ballot.

But we’ve got our work cut out for us in the months ahead — and that’s why we’re asking people in California to step up with a donation today.
