body:
[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20251227-61024&t=1001&akid=61024%2E1407376%2EIRruX5 ]Demand Justice PAC[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20251227-61024&t=1002&akid=61024%2E1407376%2EIRruX5 ]Demand Justice PAC

Right now, the far-right is stacking the courts with judges who threaten
our rights, our democracy, and the balance of power for decades to come.
Every decision, every law, every right could be reshaped if we don’t act.
And the time to act is now.

At Demand Justice, we’re standing in their way. We expose corruption in
the courts, hold lawmakers accountable — Republican and Democrat alike —
and turn grassroots energy into real, people-powered action to defend the
integrity of our judicial system.

We need to raise crucial support before the year ends. Setting the tone
now ensures we enter 2026 with the resources and momentum to fight back
against extremism and corruption from day one.

If you’ve been waiting for the right moment to join the fight, this is it. Here’s a
snapshot of where things stand for you:

You

yet!

*This data reflects donations made online with this email address. If
you've made a previous contribution via check or with a different email
address, it may not be reflected above. Thank you for supporting Demand
Justice.

Grassroots supporters are the backbone of this fight. When thousands of
people give, share, and mobilize, we gain the power to:

* Stop dangerous nominees from taking lifetime seats.
* Hold lawmakers accountable.
* Ensure our courts protect everyone’s rights, not just the privileged
few.

[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/55459?c0=dj-em-20251227.B8Uylu&refcode=em20251227-61024&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1004&akid=61024%2E1407376%2EIRruX5 ]So, will you chip in
$5 today to Demand Justice and join the fight — helping us end the year
strong, and start 2026 ready to defend our democracy?

**$10 becomes $20, $20 becomes $40, and every contribution makes twice the impact to defend democracy.**
Your generosity could go twice as far to prevent attempts at voter suppression, including mass voter purges.
Twice as far to stop the GOP from silencing voters of color through racially discriminatory redistricting.
Twice as far to elect Democracy Champions in 2026 and beyond.
And twice as far to stop the rise of autocracy in America.
We each face individual opportunities to fight for free and fair elections, and we each contribute in different ways at different times — from attending protests, to driving neighbors to the polls, to filling our group chats with voter registration links.
But if you can make a donation at this moment, it could make twice the difference to expand our collective power. And it would represent an opportunity to end 2025 not just on a high note — but on a defiant note.
We refuse to submit to authoritarianism. We refuse to give up on free and fair elections. And we are resolved to ensure the freedom to vote.

clean_body: I can’t tell you how much this means for our team | | | First and foremost, I hope you and your loved ones are enjoying a happy, healthy holiday season. As Director of Development for Fair Fight, I get to see firsthand how your generosity fuels our pro-democracy movement. Alongside supporters across the country, you make it possible to defend free and fair elections. Thank you. Secondly, I wanted to make sure you saw this exciting new chance to maximize your impact. Keep reading to see the email from Monday: = When I got the news about this match, I was overcome with gratitude. I knew that this team would step up to make sure we could take full advantage of this critical opportunity. I can’t tell you how much it means for our team to see your support — but I hope you know it makes a vital difference. = Thank you, Eva (she/her/ella) Director of Development Fair Fight | = \--------------------------Forwarded Message-------------------------- From: Fair Fight Sent: Monday, December 22, 2025 This is huge, team: from now through December 31, thanks to a generous donor,=** = $10 becomes $20, $20 becomes $40, and every contribution makes twice the impact to defend democracy. Your generosity could go twice as far to prevent attempts at voter suppression, including mass voter purges. Twice as far to stop the GOP from silencing voters of color through racially discriminatory redistricting. Twice as far to elect Democracy Champions in 2026 and beyond. And twice as far to stop the rise of autocracy in America. We each face individual opportunities to fight for free and fair elections, and we each contribute in different ways at different times — from attending protests, to driving neighbors to the polls, to filling our group chats with voter registration links. But if you can make a donation at this moment, it could make twice the difference to expand our collective power. And it would represent an opportunity to end 2025 not just on a high note — but on a defiant note. We refuse to submit to authoritarianism. We refuse to give up on free and fair elections. And we are resolved to ensure the freedom to vote. = If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will process automatically: = | = ---|--- = | = ---|--- = | = ---|--- In solidarity, The Fair Fight Team = | 1270 Caroline Street | Suite D120-430 | Atlanta, GA 30307 ©2025 Fair Fight - All rights reserved. name: Gwen Walz
subject: Protecting our Minnesota values
disclaimer_text: paid for by Walz for Governor
body:
# [](https://act.timwalz.com/go/195?refcode=em20251227-359&t=1&akid=359%2E34582%2EXO-3Ke)
Hello Peter, it's Gwen Walz.
I wanted to take a moment to reflect on what’s possible when Minnesotans show up for one another.
Together, we’ve made real progress: ensuring every student is fed breakfast and lunch at school, delivering a nation-leading child tax credit, and protecting reproductive freedom, among so much else. These aren’t just accomplishments we’re proud of — they’re wins worth defending.
Next year, all of it will be on the ballot. And it’s up to us to stand up for the future we’ve built for our children.
**As we approach our first — and only — public deadline of the year, I’m asking you to help us reach our $100,000 goal and lay a strong foundation for the fight ahead.**
**[Please consider donating $5 to help us reach our end-of-year goal, reelect Tim, and protect Minnesota’s future.](https://act.timwalz.com/go/73?c0=em_tw_20251227_1.P37nud&amount=5&refcode=em20251227-359&t=2&akid=359%2E34582%2EXO-3Ke)**
**[Donate $5 ››](https://act.timwalz.com/go/73?c0=em_tw_20251227_1.P37nud&amount=5&refcode=20251227-359&t=21&akid=359%2E34582%2EXO-3Ke)**
Thank you for believing in this work and our shared Minnesota values. It truly means more than I can say.
With gratitude,
Gwen

**[Donate ››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_tw_footer?refcode=em20251227-359&t=22&refcode2=359_34582_XO-3Ke&akid=359%2E34582%2EXO-3Ke)**

name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: A small thank you from me
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople
body:
# [](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/43706?t=1&akid=240564%2E444211%2EoMrci0)
When I’m traveling Texas—talking with folks where they're at, registering voters on college campuses, answering questions at a town hall, or signing up new volunteers—I’m reminded of the fact that _our democracy works best when we bring more people into it._
This is why we built Powered by People. Not to chase headlines or win the social media wars, but to show up everywhere, listen to everyone, and help people take part in shaping the future of their communities and our state.
As we head into a big 2026, with a lot of work ahead of us, I wanted to reach out and see if I could send you a note in the mail.
[**If you chip in any amount today, I’ll send you a postcard as a small token of my appreciation.**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-202512-postcard.EeYznG&refcode=ema20251227-240564&t=3&akid=240564%2E444211%2EoMrci0)
**[GET YOUR POSTCARD](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-202512-postcard.EeYznG&refcode=ema20251227-240564&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=4&akid=240564%2E444211%2EoMrci0)**
Your support helps us keep organizing in places that are too often overlooked or taken for granted. It helps register new voters, train volunteers, fight voter suppression, and build the kind of long-term people power that lasts beyond any single election cycle.
By pitching in, you’ll help fuel our 2026 plans as we organize on the ground to win critical races up and down the ballot—and you’ll get a physical postcard in the mail.
[**If you’re able to chip in today, I’d love to send you a note and keep this work moving forward.**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-202512-postcard.EeYznG&refcode=ema20251227-240564&t=5&akid=240564%2E444211%2EoMrci0)
**[GET YOUR POSTCARD](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-202512-postcard.EeYznG&refcode=ema20251227-240564&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=6&akid=240564%2E444211%2EoMrci0)**
Thank you,
Beto

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end.

This is why we built Powered by People. Not to chase headlines or win the social media wars, but to show up everywhere, listen to everyone, and help people take part in shaping the future of their communities and our state. As we head into a big 2026, with a lot of work ahead of us, I wanted to reach out and see if I could send you a note in the mail. Your support helps us keep organizing in places that are too often overlooked or taken for granted. It helps register new voters, train volunteers, fight voter suppression, and build the kind of long-term people power that lasts beyond any single election cycle. By pitching in, you’ll help fuel our 2026 plans as we organize on the ground to win critical races up and down the ballot—and you’ll get a physical postcard in the mail. Thank you, Beto

name: Chris Pappas
subject: I have bad news
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
This is not what we wanted to hear today.
#

Hey friend, it’s Mark Kelly. My friend Chris Pappas is running for Senate in New Hampshire and needs our help. I hope you’ll read the message below from him, and [**rush a split donation between Chris' campaign and mine.**](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=partner_ms_cp_em_kelly.9qM-X9&refcode=em251227-1-90&t=1&akid=6312%2E6688874%2ETcV1fk)
---
Hi team, it’s Chris, and I have some bad news.
Another Republican right-wing super PAC, AFP Action, has endorsed my opponent, John Sununu, and is preparing to spend a ton of money in this race.
[**Here’s why this matters: I’m not taking a dime from corporate PACs, so I need your help to fight back. Will you split $3 between my campaign and Mark Kelly so we can stand up to outside spending and defend New Hampshire’s Senate seat?**](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=partner_ms_cp_em_kelly.9qM-X9&refcode=em251227-1-90&t=2&akid=6312%2E6688874%2ETcV1fk)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_
| | **[Donate $3](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1396?c0=partner_ms_cp_em_kelly.bKir6A&refcode=em251227-1-90&t=21&akid=6312%2E6688874%2ETcV1fk)**
---
****
| **[Donate $25](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1397?c0=partner_ms_cp_em_kelly.FoQpcK&refcode=em251227-1-90&t=22&akid=6312%2E6688874%2ETcV1fk)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $50](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1390?c0=partner_ms_cp_em_kelly.ipS3S4&refcode=em251227-1-90&t=23&akid=6312%2E6688874%2ETcV1fk)**
---
****
| **[Donate $100](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1391?c0=partner_ms_cp_em_kelly.5h45gw&refcode=em251227-1-90&t=24&akid=6312%2E6688874%2ETcV1fk)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $250](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1392?c0=partner_ms_cp_em_kelly.jaPEzC&refcode=em251227-1-90&t=25&akid=6312%2E6688874%2ETcV1fk)**
---
****
| **[Other Amount](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1395?c0=partner_ms_cp_em_kelly.qp0Eng&refcode=em251227-1-90&t=26&akid=6312%2E6688874%2ETcV1fk)**
---
****
From the moment we launched this campaign, Donald Trump, MAGA Republicans, and their far-right billionaire sponsors have made it clear they will do whatever they can to influence New Hampshire’s Senate race.
I’ve won this kind of race before, and I’m going to continue fighting back. But I know from experience I can only succeed with **a strong grassroots team** and a lot more resources to win.
[**Please, if you’re able to, split a $3 donation between me and Mark Kelly to power our campaigns forward.**](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=partner_ms_cp_em_kelly.9qM-X9&refcode=em251227-1-90&t=27&akid=6312%2E6688874%2ETcV1fk)
Even if you’re not able to chip in today, thank you for reading important updates like these about my campaign.
Together,
Chris

Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can [ unsubscribe here](https://act.markkelly.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=28&akid=6312%2E6688874%2ETcV1fk).
Mark Kelly was a Captain in the U.S. Navy and astronaut. Use of his military rank, job titles and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or Department of Defense and reference to NASA does not imply endorsement by the National Aeronautics and Space Administration.

name: Katie C.
subject: I have a quick question
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Abdul for U
body:
[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=&t=1001&akid=1475%2E251025%2EhzijCV ]Abdul for US Senate [ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=&t=1002&akid=1475%2E251025%2EhzijCV ]Abdul for US Senate

Hi, Peter! It’s
Katie, Abdul’s Finance Director.

I hope you’re having a good holiday season. 💙 I’m reaching out because I
have a quick question: Can you donate $28,732?

...just kidding.

First of all, federal law wouldn’t even allow that. But really, I know the
majority of people reading this email can’t afford to donate that much at
one time. Totally valid!

You know who can afford to funnel boatloads of cash into our elections,
though?

Corporate PACs, AIPAC, and MAGA billionaires.

They’ve done it before, and they’ll definitely do it again. They’re why so
many politicians in D.C. act weird when it comes to supporting policies
like Medicare for All — they’ve got mega donors in their pocket telling
them not to!

Abdul is different. He’s the only candidate in this race who’s never taken
corporate PAC or AIPAC money, and never will.

That means Abdul’s stances won’t change depending on pressure from wealthy
donors behind closed doors.

That also means instead of relying on a couple of massive checks, we are

matters:

When thousands of people give $5 or $10, it adds up to the same power as

clean_body: [ ]Abdul for US Senate [ ]Abdul for US Senate Hi, Peter! It’s Katie, Abdul’s Finance Director. I hope you’re having a good holiday season. 💙 I’m reaching out because I have a quick question: Can you donate $28,732? ...just kidding. First of all, federal law wouldn’t even allow that. But really, I know the majority of people reading this email can’t afford to donate that much at one time. Totally valid! You know who can afford to funnel boatloads of cash into our elections, though? Corporate PACs, AIPAC, and MAGA billionaires. They’ve done it before, and they’ll definitely do it again. They’re why so many politicians in D.C. act weird when it comes to supporting policies like Medicare for All — they’ve got mega donors in their pocket telling them not to! Abdul is different. He’s the only candidate in this race who’s never taken corporate PAC or AIPAC money, and never will. That means Abdul’s stances won’t change depending on pressure from wealthy donors behind closed doors. That also means instead of relying on a couple of massive checks, we are counting on folks like you to pitch in. And here’s the part that really matters: When thousands of people give $5 or $10, it adds up to the same power as those massive checks, except it comes with accountability to real working people. body:
# [](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/18?refcode=em20251227-949&t=1&akid=958%2E294397%2EdZMrv7)
Is there *anything* we can say to earn your support today?
What if we told you that even $5 makes a difference in helping us reach Wisconsinites during these final four months of our campaign?
It’s _true._
Small-dollar gifts from thousands of supporters are how we reach more voters and push back against coordinated right-wing attacks.
Alongside that mounting pressure, there’s another big reason why we need every supporter to step up: **On December 31, we’re facing a major public fundraising deadline.**
Everything we’ve raised will be made public for our opponent and the right-wing special interests backing them to see.
If they think our grassroots coalition is losing steam, they’ll unleash a flood of dark money to take us down.
[**That’s why we’re asking: Will you pitch in $5 or more today to help elect Judge Chris Taylor and protect Wisconsin’s future?**](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/146?c0=em_ms_jct_20251227_1_ak.eSSP-l&refcode=em20251227-949&t=3&akid=958%2E294397%2EdZMrv7)
**[Donate $10 ››](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/31?c0=em_ms_jct_20251227_1_ak.Srk9GJ&amount=10&refcode=em20251227-949&t=22&akid=958%2E294397%2EdZMrv7)**
On the bench, Judge Chris Taylor has shown what real judicial integrity looks like. She’s committed to a justice system that works for people — _not powerful interests_ — which is exactly why extremist groups are so desperate to defeat her.
Thanks for taking action,
Team Taylor
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_jct_footer?refcode=em20251227-949&t=23&refcode2=958_294397_dZMrv7&akid=958%2E294397%2EdZMrv7)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

| | Friend, The Bull Elephant just spotlighted something every Virginia conservative needs to hear – and it confirms exactly what we’ve been saying: **Bryce Reeves is the conservative warrior who can finally take down Mark Warner.** _Here’s why people across Virginia are fired up:_
* **A real U.S. Army Ranger – not a career politician**
* **Former narcotics detective who fought cartels on Virginia streets**
* **Small business owner who’s lived the struggle of regulation**
* **A proven conservative who** _**delivers**_**results**
* **Endorsed by Virginia’s strongest conservative leaders**

And that’s only scratching the surface.

This is it. The final fundraising deadline of 2025. The moment that will set the stage for 2026. A chance for us to send a message.

name: Rashida Tlaib
subject: Our movement won victories in 2025, despiteRepublicans’ control over our government.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress
body:
We’re holding Trump and corporations accountable, defending our rights, and blocking abuses of power. | | | [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.X6XUC7u8ppFLNtMO_55SZlNseb7gEYIOhm_EWnhzEOZKmTtnV3k2h4V2x_O3H7rdW2az5rgktBwzxQ8Ml9Wgkmv85qW-UsBonL4KRa0bcNqdICU6T3NBDdNIZG7w7wEWWVoo7ld5hxYFJKBoB9S52TBn1ow5cYEJuSuYVHtwSIoJRzt0MCSEZaF6-BJILJ8BRxnKPvscCgYPoHsTci7fywaJDRcfll8p-E_039AZbIeh5yZRZG1Z08cdeS6Y3eWLp1KRRRDYBmZ5VQpoaoEjdF8QYVjHSoktjvesfZQcEniE-xxWeSZdsxBvPntDcGH7Jrc5A76aHRflaZNIXbwXZ1s2Ivjk43_cWIHAV4zF5Aeqdfv_twlt7CGV4R3x-0aO_fUYEt_8WEfxWX3pFSa7eWfznJi_xG_C10p6xNPORYfuH76Q8WfBMH07bhSiSNtw/4ms/uS--K6IDQN2SLNLTtQQTgg/h0/h001._pvbcYzX5mns6rwRVWNbFUJIJbMWCgzUzmrLQL_y4RY)
---
Friend, This year, our movement built power and scored victories against Republicans’ harmful and oppressive agenda. For example:
* I joined legal filings against Trump’s abuses of power, and **together we succeeded in reinstating more than 1,500 student visas, releasing detained students** , and moving federal judges to block many of Trump’s actions.
* **I more than doubled Congressional support for federal funding to replace lead pipes and clean up drinking water** , help families pay their water bills, and expand childcare benefits.
* We secured 218 signatures to **force votes on releasing the Epstein files and extending the Affordable Care Act tax credits**.
* We **pushed a record number of Congresspeople to** **renounce anti-Palestinian group AIPAC** and support an arms embargo.
* Communities across the country **blocked ICE agents from kidnapping their neighbors** , and millions of Americans came together to protest Trump. I’ve been proud to march alongside you and lift up your voices in Congress.

I’ll share more below about the investigations, congressional letters, and policies I’ve led this year to help struggling families and hold Trump accountable. But first: [**Could you make a year-end donation to my re-election campaign today so we can keep growing the movement against fascism and urgently defending our communities from attacks?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtmyMCoifRL1cgMpWm77a7hCi_QFbWxxdVFIaNshy_IQVsAMzjmaBrO7xlqQAYvji9mAkr5YnoxemLaLt5UJ129S7kN8Xq01sK4ILzdidBqHojdTMcybEwrnxmSU_52WCzvOv6BLAKvMR2i6Xv9bto07cxhUdaATOH3bdDs974eoPW0enuIDbP_A_7K-Jf7qNT75_jWC6fRpD-xwtgb-PWb6bpXnbuQZn3VKuxxvO93vaBBTRBNuLuxMkCdutrrMhU0vEtRAH7Ii4WBWBpAR1eNoi1P_7-Md72Thvgl1Nqf5z3TDnHWeSG0q5xwxcRf4a7kFPSnQJJQJLfn_-J311HIxvedWu_qCM6crGjVOxlWVL4Z3XLTTFaNFkVvtQYdZ8qB4xQsKz7qNUUJhORuCN21WRIBiobsmWh697moBeV4mUmKExC-EKaqYQItlsKOjuGjP5HQ2jgkMi8W29c9O6K4btrkjpnKOG-MvZew5q0oM_p7tALEWPFdo6kGSKNyJ4o/4ms/uS--K6IDQN2SLNLTtQQTgg/h1/h001.u0MD5lQTcnxDj6xr2in3nZ70QynEkky5P4JJ2qjzpYA) | [MAKE A YEAR-END DONATION](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtmyMCoifRL1cgMpWm77a7hCi_QFbWxxdVFIaNshy_IQVsAMzjmaBrO7xlqQAYvji81sUd17bQNy6oXC5xBA15To2FDry8jUuh6rzTPxlgh6ovmwxJ6I03-_92tS2o0jRqkoimSlLIkZ7JDR91oCNCaJOY7WJHWC-ojWdNZMHozyhNneBlca1i21T4qqeXMTs0CDRO-1Ed4fbjDY3mqsW2Dc1Cu2g4y16AY74u7LO-WP3smvQiES4y88_5YmJOazFo3DMzG1jRDb6QkNSGEHLYZP2K7t9ICcFZJ-q8R6M4pB0l1jjM96XwvatxuAyOIhegnoNcRY7TzgqBTs_4pJAuHpXs79B9JqpTHsmGU2R0XpHJ_3GpjT2DDWDmIEQddQtR53OzZj8ZcnrwGtz67n8i5tAgjJQ6fRBgvCqGAZCaO2XuO6qS-Fscy_PUavQ_CN0KV_xUKbNbXyVRJu_A9LVOYm8lj7Qs5yNOLLziuMiG8pyUV8jO026U2p6jHQztPT9Y/4ms/uS--K6IDQN2SLNLTtQQTgg/h2/h001.4cLEQ8Mp6KGfl41yRQEBByjQPP8IDHXUE2-mPYGx554)
---
This year,**I led and participated in hundreds of investigations and letters to the Trump administration** —demanding restoration of federal funding, reinstatement of environmental protections, an end to violations of immigrants’ rights, solutions for the Black maternal health crisis, and more.
I also leveraged my oversight responsibilities and my position in the House Oversight and Financial Services Committees to:
* **Visit ICE and Border Patrol facilities** to expose inhumane conditions
* **Question Big Tech leaders** about AI’s use in discriminatory price gouging
* **Question leaders in Trump’s administration** about attacks on anti-discrimination laws
* **Try to subpoena Wall Street and Elon Musk** to testify before Congress
* Introduce amendments to **prevent tax breaks for billionaires and release Epstein’s financial transactions**

And I secured significant support from my colleagues for **23 policies I introduced this year** , which would:
* Increase affordable housing, cash assistance for families and unhoused youth, civil rights protections, wages for musicians, health care providers for veterans, FEMA disaster assistance, justice for survivors of Agent Orange, and health equity
* Support more families in qualifying for federal assistance
* Stop corporations’ price gouging, reduce firearm deaths of children, prevent discrimination in car insurance rates, block weapons to Israel’s military, stop the genocide in Gaza, ban members of Congress from trading or owning stocks in companies that do business with the Department of Defense, and tax outrageous CEO pay

Many people are feeling afraid and hopeless right now, and that’s understandable—but we must not give into cynicism and despair. **We have so much power when we come together, and we’re making progress toward winning back control of Congress in 2026** and ensuring Democrats pass transformative policies for the people.

clean_body: We’re holding Trump and corporations accountable, defending our rights, and blocking abuses of power. | | | Friend, This year, our movement built power and scored victories against Republicans’ harmful and oppressive agenda. For example: I joined legal filings against Trump’s abuses of power, and together we succeeded in reinstating more than 1,500 student visas, releasing detained students , and moving federal judges to block many of Trump’s actions. I more than doubled Congressional support for federal funding to replace lead pipes and clean up drinking water , help families pay their water bills, and expand childcare benefits. We secured 218 signatures to force votes on releasing the Epstein files and extending the Affordable Care Act tax credits. We pushed a record number of Congresspeople to renounce anti-Palestinian group AIPAC and support an arms embargo. Communities across the country blocked ICE agents from kidnapping their neighbors , and millions of Americans came together to protest Trump. I’ve been proud to march alongside you and lift up your voices in Congress. I’ll share more below about the investigations, congressional letters, and policies I’ve led this year to help struggling families and hold Trump accountable. But first: | This year,I led and participated in hundreds of investigations and letters to the Trump administration —demanding restoration of federal funding, reinstatement of environmental protections, an end to violations of immigrants’ rights, solutions for the Black maternal health crisis, and more. I also leveraged my oversight responsibilities and my position in the House Oversight and Financial Services Committees to: Visit ICE and Border Patrol facilities to expose inhumane conditions Question Big Tech leaders about AI’s use in discriminatory price gouging Question leaders in Trump’s administration about attacks on anti-discrimination laws Try to subpoena Wall Street and Elon Musk to testify before Congress Introduce amendments to prevent tax breaks for billionaires and release Epstein’s financial transactions And I secured significant support from my colleagues for 23 policies I introduced this year , which would: Increase affordable housing, cash assistance for families and unhoused youth, civil rights protections, wages for musicians, health care providers for veterans, FEMA disaster assistance, justice for survivors of Agent Orange, and health equity Support more families in qualifying for federal assistance Stop corporations’ price gouging, reduce firearm deaths of children, prevent discrimination in car insurance rates, block weapons to Israel’s military, stop the genocide in Gaza, ban members of Congress from trading or owning stocks in companies that do business with the Department of Defense, and tax outrageous CEO pay Many people are feeling afraid and hopeless right now, and that’s understandable—but we must not give into cynicism and despair. We have so much power when we come together, and we’re making progress toward winning back control of Congress in 2026 and ensuring Democrats pass transformative policies for the people. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | | | Thank you so much for your support and your work to stop fascism. Together, we’ll hold Trump and corporations accountable, and we will not only defend our rights but expand them. With you in the fight, Rashida * Rashida Tlaib for Congress PO Box 32777 Detroit, MI 48232 | Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress | | Sent via . | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

| **Musk diving into 2026 midterms for the GOP** _\- Axios_
* * *
The world’s richest person is already writing massive checks to buy the 2026 midterms for Republicans. **[We're asking for just $3 before our critical End-of-Year deadline to stop him.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122068933/591477691/2105508926?amount=3&refcode=ea.597455ef-43e3-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ms=ea.597455ef-43e3-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjU5NzQ1NWVmLTQzZTMtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=UJMlGDolIHlFWL1ZNwuR2d-ov6nsyuIOsZYUsqU-M64=)** Here's why this matters: **→ Musk is ready to do whatever it takes to lock in Republican power for years to come.** He’s already cut massive checks to Republican House and Senate campaigns, and he's promised to keep the money flowing in 2026. name: Speaker Mike Johnson
subject: It’s go time
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRCC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
Peter - I wanted to inform you sooner rather than later.
I've come to truly appreciate your consistent record.
With everything you've given to our movement, there was no question in my mind that I needed to send you this message ASAP.
I'd hate to see this opportunity pass you by.
That's why as soon as the House GOP Platinum committee flagged your profile, I knew I had to extend both a message of gratitude and of urgency!

1. **THANK YOU! I am so proud of everything we have accomplished and how far you 've come in your support for the House GOP, Peter. We would not be able to deliver on our America First agenda without you!**
2. **You 're now eligible for PLATINUM STATUS! Will you please accept this status and add this critical accomplishment to your GOP legacy?**

clean_body: Peter - I wanted to inform you sooner rather than later. I've come to truly appreciate your consistent record. With everything you've given to our movement, there was no question in my mind that I needed to send you this message ASAP. I'd hate to see this opportunity pass you by. That's why as soon as the House GOP Platinum committee flagged your profile, I knew I had to extend both a message of gratitude and of urgency! 1. THANK YOU! I am so proud of everything we have accomplished and how far you 've come in your support for the House GOP, Peter. We would not be able to deliver on our America First agenda without you! 2. You 're now eligible for PLATINUM STATUS! Will you please accept this status and add this critical accomplishment to your GOP legacy? I'm not at all worried about your continued commitment. The last thing I would ever do is question your loyalty. But I'd hate for you to miss out on your chance to upgrade today with this limited time offer! [Because of your proven record, you're just [1] contribution away from a YEAR of PLATINUM STATUS!]( look forward to hearing back from you. God bless, House Speaker Mike Johnson| | Paid for by the NRCC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. | | Friend, It’s Mike Rogers.

I’m writing to you directly because I don’t know who else I can trust right now. I just left a series of meetings that shook me: the kind of conversations that never make it into press briefings or fundraising emails.

I’m not supposed to tell you what I heard.

I’m not supposed to share anything written.

[**But I wrote a personal note anyway... and it’s for you. clean_body: I’m trusting you with something sensitive. | | | | | Friend, It’s Mike Rogers. I’m writing to you directly because I don’t know who else I can trust right now. I just left a series of meetings that shook me: the kind of conversations that never make it into press briefings or fundraising emails. I’m not supposed to tell you what I heard. I’m not supposed to share anything written. Please understand: I’m asking you humbly to read it immediately. Quietly. I wouldn’t come to you like this if I had any other choice. — Mike Rogers Michigan U.S. Senate Candidate | | | Text JOIN to 22033 | |

name: Scott Fitzgerald
subject: Will You Help Us Finish Strong?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scott Fitzgerald for Congress
body:

[|
---](https://scottfitzgeraldforcongress.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c59bc0db5e945d61b14749b2b&id=bf8a0d251f&e=561a28864e)
| | | | |
---
| |

Dear Friend,As we approach the end of this cycle and look ahead to 2026, I wanted to reach out to you personally.
The next election will be here sooner than anyone thinks, and what happens between now and then will determine whether we’re ready for whatever comes our way. In Wisconsin and across the country the political landscape is shifting. District lines could change, outside groups are preparing to spend millions in Wisconsin to take me and my colleagues down, and control of the U.S. House may once again come down to a handful of races like ours.
I’m proud of the work we’ve done together. Standing up for Wisconsin values, fighting for fiscal responsibility, and pushing back against Washington overreach. But staying competitive takes resources, preparation, and a strong team behind us.
This is my final fundraising appeal before we turn the page toward 2026. If you’re able, I hope you’ll consider [clicking here and contributing](https://scottfitzgeraldforcongress.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c59bc0db5e945d61b14749b2b&id=57adc84657&e=561a28864e) [**$10, $35, $50, $100, $500 or anything you can do**](https://scottfitzgeraldforcongress.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c59bc0db5e945d61b14749b2b&id=6ee890c8c4&e=561a28864e) to help us close out this cycle strong and prepare for the fight ahead.
Every dollar helps us stay prepared, communicate our message, and ensure we’re ready to win no matter what the political environment looks like.
Thank you for your continued support and your belief in the work we’re doing. I’m grateful to have you on our team.
Sincerely,
**Scott Fitzgerald**
Member of Congress
**P.S.**[**I hope you can click here to donate now and help us finish strong before the end of the year!**](https://scottfitzgeraldforcongress.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c59bc0db5e945d61b14749b2b&id=b13db8edc2&e=561a28864e)

| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw

Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

I’m reaching out to let you know that I’ve endorsed Randy Villegas for Congress in California’s 22nd District, and to ask you to support his campaign with a **[donation of $27](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122070008/591481562/-1934691120?refcode=em-12.27.25-all-rs&amount=27&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTc1MjUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZGM1ODgzODYtNDVlMy1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=GU_1-eFF0zsllCL6pn8H_QtFWW9e4jlUFxCAR7OZpZI=)** today. Here’s why your support matters: At a time when the billionaire class is raking in record profits while millions of Americans are living paycheck to paycheck, we need working-class fighters in Congress who will take on the corporate greed that’s rigged our system. **Randy Villegas is one of those fighters.** I am proud to endorse Randy because we need more working-class people in Congress who are not beholden to the corporations and special interests that have rigged our political system in favor of the billionaire political class. Randy is an educator and small business owner from a working class family who understands the struggles that so many millions of Americans are facing everyday. He is rejecting corporate PAC money because he understands the outsized influence that big corporations have on our political process and that we need to return the power to the people. Now he’s running in one of the most competitive races in the country against a Republican incumbent backed by those very same corporate interests and billionaire donors who’ve rigged the system for themselves. If we want to flip this seat and send a working-class champion to Congress, we need to show that grassroots power can beat big money. **[That’s why I’m asking: Will you contribute $27 today to help elect Randy Villegas and build the campaign it’ll take to win?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122070024/591481593/-1934691120?refcode=em-12.27.25-all-rs&amount=27&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTc1MjUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZGM1ODgzODYtNDVlMy1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=GU_1-eFF0zsllCL6pn8H_QtFWW9e4jlUFxCAR7OZpZI=)** __If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: __ | | | [Chip in $10 Now](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122070031/591481615/-1934691120?refcode=em-12.27.25-all-rs&express_lane=true&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTc1MjUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZGM1ODgzODYtNDVlMy1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=GU_1-eFF0zsllCL6pn8H_QtFWW9e4jlUFxCAR7OZpZI=)

name: Emily Randall
subject: Emily's Notes: Man, I feel like a legislator! 🎵
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Emily Randall for Congress
body:
My team and I have been closely monitoring the flooding across Western Washington.

| Peter, welcome to the latest edition of Emily’s Notes! **FIRST OFF:** My team and I have been closely monitoring the flooding across Western Washington. I visited the Lake Sylvia Dam and Chehalis River with local, state, and tribal leaders to make sure we were “boots on the ground” alongside them. Our state’s delegation urged President Trump to issue an [_**Emergency Declaration**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122070316/591482217/1964332620?hl=en&img_index=1&refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.12.27_DecNewsletter&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MjY2ZmRhOC00NWUzLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Xji74f9uQWB5rnJ08FzgmA7HqQ5Mg1rfu8PiniPezVQ=&emci=a5d76b48-c7e2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=7266fda8-45e3-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=23483442), which unlocks federal funds and assistance for disaster relief. The situation is moving quickly as I write this, but we’re doing everything we can to keep our neighbors safe and secure the resources to rebuild. Some more updates from Washington’s 6th and Washington, D.C.: **• Does anyone recognize this guy? 😁** At a gathering with freshman congressmembers hosted by Freshman Class President Rep. Yassamin Ansari, I finally met President Barack Obama. I think my face says it all. He spoke to us about public service and how we can meet this moment. The evening left me feeling, say it with me: _fired up and ready to go!_ **• Man, I feel like a legislator!** – I passed [_**three bills**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122070318/591482227/-545365986?hl=en&refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.12.27_DecNewsletter&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MjY2ZmRhOC00NWUzLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Xji74f9uQWB5rnJ08FzgmA7HqQ5Mg1rfu8PiniPezVQ=&emci=a5d76b48-c7e2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=7266fda8-45e3-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=23483442) off the House floor this month! The _Lower Elwha Klallam Tribe Project Lands Restoration Act_ and _Quinault Indian Nation Land Transfer Act_ uphold our treaty obligations and transfer land back to Tribal ownership. The _Protecting America’s Workforce Act_ restores collective bargaining rights to thousands of federal workers. Add to that hours of managing floor debate ([_**along with my Republican colleague Jeff Hurd of Colorado**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122070319/591482243/473580658?hl=en&refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.12.27_DecNewsletter&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MjY2ZmRhOC00NWUzLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Xji74f9uQWB5rnJ08FzgmA7HqQ5Mg1rfu8PiniPezVQ=&emci=a5d76b48-c7e2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=7266fda8-45e3-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=23483442)), and I feel like an actual legislator in a way I haven’t for much of this year. We’re not here to perform for cameras, we’re here to make policy!!! **• My 17th and final town hall of 202** 5 — We held our third tele-town hall this month, and 14,000 (!!) constituents joined, asking questions about SNAP, healthcare premiums, and holding officials like Pete Hegseth and Kristi Noem accountable. The Trump administration needs to know that people are watching – and we want leaders who uphold the Constitution. If you missed it, [_**the video of the full town hall is on YouTube.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122070324/591482249/-803653937?v=YwRwWWw7X7A&refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.12.27_DecNewsletter&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MjY2ZmRhOC00NWUzLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Xji74f9uQWB5rnJ08FzgmA7HqQ5Mg1rfu8PiniPezVQ=&emci=a5d76b48-c7e2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=7266fda8-45e3-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=23483442) **• Speaking of Kristi Noem** – DHS Secretary Kristi Noem came to the Hill to testify in front of Congress. clean_body: My team and I have been closely monitoring the flooding across Western Washington. | Peter, welcome to the latest edition of Emily’s Notes! FIRST OFF: My team and I have been closely monitoring the flooding across Western Washington. I visited the Lake Sylvia Dam and Chehalis River with local, state, and tribal leaders to make sure we were “boots on the ground” alongside them. Our state’s delegation urged President Trump to issue an , which unlocks federal funds and assistance for disaster relief. The situation is moving quickly as I write this, but we’re doing everything we can to keep our neighbors safe and secure the resources to rebuild. Some more updates from Washington’s 6th and Washington, D.C.: • Does anyone recognize this guy? 😁 At a gathering with freshman congressmembers hosted by Freshman Class President Rep. Yassamin Ansari, I finally met President Barack Obama. I think my face says it all. He spoke to us about public service and how we can meet this moment. The evening left me feeling, say it with me: fired up and ready to go! • Man, I feel like a legislator! – I passed off the House floor this month! The Lower Elwha Klallam Tribe Project Lands Restoration Act and Quinault Indian Nation Land Transfer Act uphold our treaty obligations and transfer land back to Tribal ownership. The Protecting America’s Workforce Act restores collective bargaining rights to thousands of federal workers. Add to that hours of managing floor debate (), and I feel like an actual legislator in a way I haven’t for much of this year. We’re not here to perform for cameras, we’re here to make policy!!! • My 17th and final town hall of 202 5 — We held our third tele-town hall this month, and 14,000 (!!) constituents joined, asking questions about SNAP, healthcare premiums, and holding officials like Pete Hegseth and Kristi Noem accountable. The Trump administration needs to know that people are watching – and we want leaders who uphold the Constitution. If you missed it, • Speaking of Kristi Noem – DHS Secretary Kristi Noem came to the Hill to testify in front of Congress. I was on my way to hold her accountable for having ICE agents use attack dogs and ask about the inhumane treatment of detainees at the Tacoma Detention Center, We should be using taxpayer dollars to help families dealing with the floods in Washington, not to Now is the part where I ask you for an end-of-year donation. As I write this, three Republicans just broke ranks and joined Democrats to force a vote on extending ACA healthcare funding. All it took was three. And with retirements and special elections coming up, that margin could shrink. It’s no wonder Trump and his team are begging red states to gerrymander their maps, picking their voters before the voters have the chance to pick someone else! I sound like a broken record, but taking back the House next year is priority #1. We need leaders who understand their job is to check and balance the Executive Branch, not rubber-stamp everything they ask for. In 2025, thanks to your support, we were able to lend early support to Democrats in swing districts. That includes a PAC donation to JoAnna Mendoza in Arizona’s 6th and additional funding for the DCCC, the House Democrats’ political arm. = If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = = = = = Thank you so much for your patience, enthusiasm, and support during this wild year. She grew up in a working-class Union household in Port Orchard and was the first in her family to attend college. After serving in the State Senate and passing nearly 200 bills, Emily made history in 2024 as the first queer Latina elected to Congress. Now, Emily is fighting for WA working families in D.C. and organizing to take back the House. name: Squad Victory Fund
subject: As the year draws to a close
body:
[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/18910?refcode=em20251227-1769-foote&t=1001&akid=1769%2E342411%2EelfdwV ]Squad Victory Fund

Peter,

As the year draws to a close, we need to be honest. We’re currently
tracking behind our fundraising goal. And that matters — a lot.

Every dollar we raise supports our progressive leaders in Congress who are
fighting for real change — leaders who stand up for justice, equity, and
policies that put people first.

Falling behind means less support for these champions going into an
election year, and less power to challenge the status quo in Congress.

We cannot let that happen. The stakes are too high, and the opposition is
relentless.

Will you help us close the gap before the year ends? Your support makes
all the difference in strengthening our movement — a movement that will
move our country forward.

Please chip in $3 today to keep our momentum strong ahead of
Wednesday’s deadline. You can use this link here to make a contribution:

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20251227-movement.hsXv_x&personal=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=em20251227-1769&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=1769%2E342411%2EelfdwV ]https://secure.actblue.com/donate/svfem-20251227-movement?personal=dpwillis67@gmail.com

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20251227-movement.hsXv_x&refcode=em20251227-1769&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1021&akid=1769%2E342411%2EelfdwV ]Contribute ››

In solidarity,

The Squad Victory Fund

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe:

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.squadvictoryfund.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=1769%2E342411%2EelfdwV

Paid for by The Squad Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

In addition to what’s legally required, The Squad Victory Fund does not accept contributions from: (1) corporations, associations, or their PACs; (2) individuals registered as federal lobbyists or under FARA; (3) from the PACs, lobbyists, or SEC-named executives of fossil fuel companies; or (4) from the Fraternal Order of Police.

Contributions or gifts to The Squad Victory Fund are not tax deductible.

Contributions to The Squad Victory Fund will be allocated equally among: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

Any contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if any contributor makes a contribution that, when allocated, would exceed the amount that the contributor may lawfully give to any participant.

After contributing to The Squad Victory Fund, you will automatically be signed up to receive email updates from Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, and Rashida Tlaib for Congress. You may of course opt-out by unsubscribing at any time.

To contribute via check, please address to The Squad Victory Fund c/o MBA Consulting Group, PO Box 15845, Washington D.C., 20003

Email us: info@squadvictoryfund.com

disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CHRISTINA BOHANNAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
Santa sometimes sends a few of us elves out to keep an eye on political campaigns heading into election season.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122071155/591484880/-975735282?refcode=em_logoheader&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMTRlZTEzMi00NmUzLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=gRr1YFLmHJMBOty8wtX1VHAe2sD6tFUlFV9vY56jPeg=)

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122071156/591484881/-1610961223?refcode=em25_251227_eoq2_fndd_gq_act___hollyelf_ettx&&&&utm_campaign=em25_251227_eoq2_fndd_gq_act___hollyelf_ettx&&&&utm_medium=email&&&&refcode2=014ee132-46e3-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMTRlZTEzMi00NmUzLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=gRr1YFLmHJMBOty8wtX1VHAe2sD6tFUlFV9vY56jPeg=)

Peter, I know what you're thinking: _“An elf on the shelf?! Emailing me?! After Christmas?!”_ Fair! Normally I'd be back at the North Pole right now. **Technically...I should've been on that return sleigh. But instead, I'm here in Iowa.**

Santa sometimes sends a few of us elves out to keep an eye on political campaigns heading into election season. When I climbed up to the tallest shelf in Bohannan HQ, I saw two things immediately: The campaign manager, Dan, _definitely_ sneaks more than his fair share of gingerbread cookies. But most importantly, **this team is working harder than Santa's reindeer on Christmas Eve to elect Christina Bohannan.**

As an elf, I believe in peace on earth, jolly justice, and doing good deeds for others. Christina believes in all of that, AND she's got amazing plans to make life better for folks across Iowa's 1st District.

A few days ago, I started to pack for my trip back home (I even had my tiny snow boots on)...but I couldn't shake the feeling that I could do more good right here. **So I wrote Santa a very polite resignation letter and officially became the jolliest co-director of outreach Iowa has ever seen.**

And now I need your help to keep the momentum merry and bright. **[Will you chip in today toward Christina's $25,000 end-of-year goal to help strengthen her campaign? Every donation — big or elf-sized — helps us get one step closer to sending Christina to Washington to do good for her fellow Iowans.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122071158/591484883/-1610961223?refcode=em25_251227_eoq2_fndd_gq_act___hollyelf_ettx&&&&utm_campaign=em25_251227_eoq2_fndd_gq_act___hollyelf_ettx&&&&utm_medium=email&&&&refcode2=014ee132-46e3-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMTRlZTEzMi00NmUzLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=gRr1YFLmHJMBOty8wtX1VHAe2sD6tFUlFV9vY56jPeg=)**

Thank you,

Holly the Elf 🎄
Co-Director of Outreach
Team Bohannan

Christina Bohannan is a mom, law professor, engineer and former state representative. She's running for Congress to defeat Mariannette Miller-Meeks and flip IA-01 blue.

Chip in to support her campaign today >> | **[DONATE NOW »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122071160/591484885/-1610961223?refcode=em25_251227_eoq2_fndd_gq_act___hollyelf_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_251227_eoq2_fndd_gq_act___hollyelf_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=014ee132-46e3-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMTRlZTEzMi00NmUzLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=gRr1YFLmHJMBOty8wtX1VHAe2sD6tFUlFV9vY56jPeg=)**

clean_body: Santa sometimes sends a few of us elves out to keep an eye on political campaigns heading into election season. | = = Peter, I know what you're thinking: “An elf on the shelf?! Emailing me?! After Christmas?!” Fair! Normally I'd be back at the North Pole right now. Technically...I should've been on that return sleigh. But instead, I'm here in Iowa. Santa sometimes sends a few of us elves out to keep an eye on political campaigns heading into election season. When I climbed up to the tallest shelf in Bohannan HQ, I saw two things immediately: The campaign manager, Dan, definitely sneaks more than his fair share of gingerbread cookies. But most importantly, this team is working harder than Santa's reindeer on Christmas Eve to elect Christina Bohannan. As an elf, I believe in peace on earth, jolly justice, and doing good deeds for others. Christina believes in all of that, AND she's got amazing plans to make life better for folks across Iowa's 1st District. A few days ago, I started to pack for my trip back home (I even had my tiny snow boots on)...but I couldn't shake the feeling that I could do more good right here. So I wrote Santa a very polite resignation letter and officially became the jolliest co-director of outreach Iowa has ever seen. And now I need your help to keep the momentum merry and bright. = Thank you, Holly the Elf 🎄 Co-Director of Outreach Team Bohannan Christina Bohannan is a mom, law professor, engineer and former state representative. She's running for Congress to defeat Mariannette Miller-Meeks and flip IA-01 blue. Chip in to support her campaign today >> | = &recurring=auto&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMTRlZTEzMi00NmUzLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=gRr1YFLmHJMBOty8wtX1VHAe2sD6tFUlFV9vY56jPeg= | PAID FOR BY CHRISTINA BOHANNAN FOR CONGRESS P.O. "I was surprised to find that the densely packed packages were easy to prepare and were tasty as well. I definitely recommend having survival food on hand for the times when 'life' happens while we are planning other things."_ _**Billy H.**
"Received my food kit, and I was able to make the potato soup on my cookstove. Just needed boiling water. Gave it a good stir and let it simmer. When I opened up the pot, it smelled so good. You can see the chunks of potatoes and carrots in each bite you take. It's a good soup."_ _**Kevin S.**
"Perfect starter kit for any scenario. Put one in your car, work bag, in your house or RV. If you want to start preparing safe food, this is where to start."_ _**Carol B.**
"I have been iced in this weekend. I thought I would try my potato soup. To my utter delight, IT WAS DELICIOUS. Thank you for such a wonderful product and the peace of mind it brings."_ _**Ken K.**
"I am not a full-blown survivalist. I am not an idiot, either. I have been through enough in my life and have seen friends who have been through an emergency situation. Save up if you have to, but get at least a month's supply. It tastes good. Although if it's a dire of an emergency, you will be happy to eat anything. IF you have something to eat for your family. We made some of this product, and had family and friends over for them to taste. They all agreed they didn't think anything would be this good, and they will be ordering. Just do something. You can't miss out on this deal."_ _**Justin A.**
"My wife and I tried the food and we were both surprised about how good it tasted and how satisfied it made us feel afterwards. It feels good knowing that I can provide for my family if a crisis arose and I intend to get more in the future. Also the shipping and customer service has been top notch. This is probably the cheapest survival food I have found, and the company is great."_ _**Gary M.**
"I actually had lost my job and was homeless for a while. I dug into my food supply, and I cannot fully describe how delicious and easy to prepare everything is. I felt like I was eating like a king. I am going to stock up again as soon as I settle into my new job. Everyone should participate with this company. You will not be disappointed."_ _**John H.**
"We're in Florida and have made many preparations for the aftermath of a possible hurricane. While we are thankful that Florida has not been hit in several years, it gives us great peace of mind to know we have our 4Patriots kit stashed away, knowing it's not a case of "if" but "when"."_ [**Click here to claim your exclusive deal on these 4-Week Buy 1 Get 1 FREE Survival Food Kits while we still have inventory.**](https://trk.thedcbugle.com/?xtl=90l5xvutuvnijxsrfdvhsgjyre22ieghp6cdjcrrdmj7j0tvoh2e1z290whcgmhdz7hufx0zub8z14qjzgi9tqe33pq5c9bvammnhrvqlhfjoc4r4j1y6rpwpg5raqsmq6xjk8miwxyiixziwrlvcjme6mw3cfgv9r4fajbzzuxo1ccqa9ou0su5oae8shjjzpkcglzvrvanyf7o0z2dq22stg6e5dbrkl70c1fjfzwfr0j95&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t7xuam&__onlt=h) [](https://trk.thedcbugle.com/?xtl=90l5xvutuvnijxsrfdvhsgjyre22ieghp6cdjcrrdmj7j0tvoh2e1z290whcgmhdz7hufx0zub8z14qjzgi9tqe33pq5c9bvammnhrvqlhfjoc4r4j1y6rpwpg5raqsmq6xjk8miwxyiixziwrlvcjme6mw3cfgv9r4fajbzzuxo1ccqa9ou0su5oae8shjjzpkcglzvrvanyf7o0z2dq22stg6e5dbrkl70c1fjfzwfr0j95&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t7xuam&__onlt=h)

subject: It wasn't an easy decision to make

body:
#

Hey, it’s Ruben. The most important thing we can do right now is win elections. I’m sending over an email from Katie Hobbs, and I’d appreciate you taking a look and chipping in if you can.
---

Peter, it’s Katie Hobbs. I want to tell you about an important decision I made a few years ago.
**If you’re short on time and want to skip ahead to split a donation between me and Ruben Gallego, you can use this link:[**https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/RG-KH-1225**](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1000?c0=ruben-hobbs-em-split.OtSY65&refcode=em251226-1&t=1&akid=4052%2E734936%2Eb_0wuq)
_Otherwise, keep reading:_
During the 2020 election, I was Arizona’s Secretary of State. I had a job to do: **Count every vote.**
Donald Trump and the extremists surrounding him didn’t make it easy. Like millions of Americans, I watched as an enraged crowd breached the halls of our nation’s Capitol in Washington, D.C. to overturn the people’s vote.
**When the “Stop the Steal” movement took hold, I was harassed and even received death threats because of my role in overseeing Arizona’s elections.**
Some would believe that in moments like these, you only have two options — fight, or give in. **I chose to get the job done.** We counted every vote, and in the middle of a once-in-a-century pandemic, we proved that democracy works.
But I knew the work wasn’t over. It was clear that the MAGA right wouldn’t offer any real solutions; they’d just offer more and more conspiracy theories and scapegoats.
So I decided to launch my campaign for governor to deliver transparency, accountability, and results for Arizonans. My opponents and national Republicans spent tens of millions of dollars and lobbed baseless attacks to try and defeat me, but thanks to hundreds of thousands of grassroots supporters chipping in what they could, we won with less than 1% of the vote.
That brings us to _today._
Three years later, we’ve delivered incredible progress for Arizonans — lowering healthcare costs, investing in public education, and cutting taxes for middle-class families, just to name a few things. But there’s still more work to do.
I’m running to deliver another four years of progress like this, but Donald Trump, my three GOP opponents, and the national Republican establishment are determined to unseat me and flip this seat **red**.
Experts are calling the 2026 Arizona governor’s race a **pure toss-up** , just like they did in 2022. But I’m telling you now — this race is going to be even more competitive and even more expensive than the first time.
I know it might feel early to be reading emails like this, but these are the stakes. This is the level of competition in this race. And if we’re going to win this, we have to build a serious grassroots campaign from the very start.
[**So please, split $10, $25, or whatever amount you’re able to my campaign and Ruben’s to help me win this 50/50 race, defend our hard-won progress, and serve another four years as governor of Arizona. I can’t do this without you!**](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1000?c0=ruben-hobbs-em-split.OtSY65&refcode=em251226-1&t=2&akid=4052%2E734936%2Eb_0wuq)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._
| | **[Donate $5](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1061?c0=ruben-hobbs-em-split.H74Rpa&refcode=em251226-1&t=21&akid=4052%2E734936%2Eb_0wuq)**
---
****
| **[Donate $25](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1046?c0=ruben-hobbs-em-split.ekt75g&refcode=em251226-1&t=22&akid=4052%2E734936%2Eb_0wuq)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $50](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1039?c0=ruben-hobbs-em-split.fIfbgl&refcode=em251226-1&t=23&akid=4052%2E734936%2Eb_0wuq)**
---
****
| **[Donate $100](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1040?c0=ruben-hobbs-em-split.9bnlyY&refcode=em251226-1&t=24&akid=4052%2E734936%2Eb_0wuq)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $250](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1041?c0=ruben-hobbs-em-split.hbt7PN&refcode=em251226-1&t=25&akid=4052%2E734936%2Eb_0wuq)**
---
****
| **[Other Amount](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1044?c0=ruben-hobbs-em-split.ZAIK9s&refcode=em251226-1&t=26&akid=4052%2E734936%2Eb_0wuq)**
---
****
Thank you!
Katie

Paid For By Gallego for Arizona
Use of military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform do not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Emails like this are a critical tool for our campaign's fundraising and organizing efforts. If you'd like to receive only the most important emails going forward, [tell us at this link](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/signup/fewer_emails/?source=fewer-emails&t=27&akid=4052%2E734936%2Eb_0wuq). Or, if you need to, you can [unsubscribe here](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=28&akid=4052%2E734936%2Eb_0wuq). If you would like to support Ruben's campaign for Senate, please consider [making a donation today.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ruben-em-footer?refcode=em-footer&t=29&refcode2=4052_734936_b_0wuq&akid=4052%2E734936%2Eb_0wuq)

name: Elizabeth Guzman
subject: Friend, thank you. Now let’s get ready for 2026!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Elizabeth Guzman
body:
| | | Friend — Before we charge into 2026 and into the next fight, I want to pause and say something simple, but important: _Thank you._ 2025 was not an easy year. It demanded persistence, courage, and belief. And because of supporters like you, we met that moment head-on. You knocked doors, made calls, sent texts, and chipped in when it mattered. **You showed up for a vision of Virginia that is fairer, stronger, and more inclusive.** _Because of you, we protected hard-won progress.__
Because of you, Virginia remained a bulwark against extremism.
__Because of you, people who are too often ignored now have someone fighting for them._ Simply put, we blew the doors off that election in November! And I am deeply grateful. ******And now, we turn the page.** 2026 is already on the horizon — and it will be decisive. Virginia will once again be at the center of the national fight, and the work we do now will determine how strong we are when that moment arrives. The truth is: momentum doesn’t carry itself. It’s built — early, intentionally, and together. **[That’s why I’m asking you to help us start 2026 on solid ground. Can you chip in today to help us build the foundation for the year ahead?](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvhsqD6hnNNbCP7JMDskKO3DcwO3FPpifEsk6R5UXJAqeoZfkhx0rJYxvzeQyPAOXxPzaHpN7uxnA66Ao0W-7EqZyFw6iLW1nc6uNsMh9U_6HF4v65VHENXKXmwg_p7czJ39H_Oi_xKNbkK-SwGNhuoRMZ4fV8N52Q7USR41MiR14uf_p7vd7_i0ANJK6J9ZW5zTjCHfue-yt2sEn5u5JZZDJXQAHiIH2MByOQY3IitWSLeU7kgn22z6_M5S_VUBOR-0In6Dc_lgwPwA9KHWbb5Yk0Dk2YWBSwj7DgzaWvXLBvS8ljFDHS-NllbVzaJUmhrdukIIrKdZwuZwAf-dkuk5b8aAlBY3gfc50Y4K86GtA/4ms/D27WAR85Tw-8yLoFtSwRKg/h0/h001.1-roMAik24Mi9Zsv--mrV3oIKpOM9AP3iK3RlrxLodg)**********_If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately._ [Donate $10
