name: Team Voter Action Project
subject: Trump and MAGA want to kill mail-in voting
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
Democracy Docket: With New Order, Trump Tries to Kill Off Vote By Mail [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=122725_2&amount=25]
We're going to keep hammering this home, Team, because the mainstream media continues to ignore this executive order and how it will change our elections forever.
President Donald Trump's massive anti-voting executive order has sent lawmakers, election officials, and voting rights advocates into a frenzy. One provision focuses on a goal that for years has been a borderline obsession for MAGA: Cracking down on voting by mail .
For background: After every election in which he's been a candidate or in power, Trump has raged over the use of mail-in ballots in general and ballot deadlines in particular . He's tried to claim fraud for every single ballot counted after Election Day.
So of course, this order directs Attorney General Pam Bondi to “ take all necessary action ” against any states that count absentee or mail-in ballots received after Election Day.
This order makes it clear that the MAGA crew will do anything they can to codify their power permanently and silence the will of the people by suppressing and eliminating the votes we cast.
We've got to keep fighting and work to protect our elections and our fundamental right to vote. Step up with $25 or whatever you can today and help us defend vote by mail across the country >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=122725_2&amount=25]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=122725_2&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=122725_2&amount=25] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=122725_2&amount=50]
GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=122725_2&amount=100] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=122725_2&amount=250] OTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=122725_2&amount=35]
We all know that mail-in voting laws are about ballot accessibility, making it easier for voters to participate in our elections . Elderly voters, the disabled, our military service members -- they all vote by mail.
Mail-in voting has happened dating back to the Civil War. It's always been safe and secure, mail-in voting fraud is supremely rare. States have spent millions of taxpayer dollars investigating voter fraud since 2020's "big lie" and found virtually nothing. It's been used widely in some states for decades and in most of the country since the pandemic.
Our partners at VoteAmerica said it best: “ There is no good-faith reason to limit vote-by-mail access, reject ballots without postmarks, or refuse to count ballots that arrive after Election Day. If the administration moves forward with these proposals, they will effectively disenfranchise older voters, rural voters, and military voters. ”
Opponents of voting rights have proven they'll go to any length necessary to stop people from voting and make sure that they stay in power. Will you chip in $25 or whatever you can to fight their unending desire for control and protect mail-in voting? >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=122725_2&amount=25]
https://voteractionproject.org/ [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=122725_2&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=122725_2&amount=20]
Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). Integrity First Voter Action Project is organized and operates under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code.
A copy of Integrity First Voter Action Project's latest financial report may be obtained by mail at Integrity First Voter Action Project, c/o Mele Brengarth & Assoc, PO Box 15845, Washington, DC 20003.
Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www.voteractionproject.org, and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
Integrity First Voter Action Project
P.O. Box 19294
Portland, OR 97280
United States
This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Emails are a crucial way to keep supporters up to date on the latest attacks on voting rights and our efforts to expand ballot access for all Americans. If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.everyaction.com/p/FQb7Rbo110qLPmSvMAn63Q2?unsubscribedata=WMhC5KoQTQWdJ9oBql50zemTc6HfSlKLLDuYzKxeNC7bd2PhLLDGaqluEKUuLTDeW13irKAVgIzn2Bf1BmJ%2Bt2eLrSMHK76%2F9xev0bdvYDEE5bKUV%2F6XtG%2FZcWqSE80zzzmcdJbao7tdESkvzHDAf%2B3%2BtxUrOqkcG7L%2B40Tm05nWG18q6pFvOI5AcK5BzZDCBE7qcyFsi2wL%2FLhG%2B0xPG4PhdA3U8sC64R8wu8QIJq%2FrfD%2FquO3%2FpcDEQqjzquXlaObFU7tQoKJodG0DzNTrcKBVq8D4qwZaykA6bUUYJB7%2BWIv5%2F14S6VOl4LuT9EQ382Pgwx%2B%2Bai8ySilTZ7E77oa%2FKaphjqYGEIDK6UfQtZJrD4xx%2Fl8nbd8l6h4SzerU7OsgiE%2BA2wVfWt7WPEJcd2q%2BGdikAZAOlXKa0dWGQtc%3D .

name: The Liberty Ledger
subject: Crockett Discusses Her Authenticity
body:
To paraphrase the old line, “The key to political success is sincerity—if you can fake that, you’ve got it made.” And that’s where Rep. Jasmine Crockett, currently mounting a high-profile run for U.S. Senate in Texas, seems to find herself stuck in neutral. name: Cisco Aguilar
subject: Introducing myself
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Democratic Association of Secretaries of State, Treasurer Denise Merrill
body:
| Hi friend, This is Cisco Aguilar, Nevada’s Secretary of State – the state’s top election official. In 2022, Nevadans elected me to this office after I defeated one of the most extreme election deniers in the country – in a razor-tight race decided by fewer than 25,000 votes. It was a close and hard-fought election, and it showed just how much pressure our democracy is under. Since then, I’ve focused on doing the job Nevadans trusted me to do. That has meant a lot of work on modernization, innovation, and transparency. We’ve implemented a new election management system with the latest in technology and cybersecurity; started to replace our outdated corporate filings system; and made things easier for small business owners in Nevada. We even cut hold times when you call the office from 45 minutes to under 5! We’ve also worked to improve the basics: strengthening our election systems, supporting county clerks and local officials, and pushing back against misinformation meant to confuse or intimidate voters. **Now, I’m up for re-election.** Because Nevada is a swing state, the pressure on our election system is always high – and extremists have shown they’re willing to exploit that. The person elected Secretary of State this year will be responsible for running the 2028 election Imagine an election-denier in this role. That’s not a hypothetical we can afford to test. And in a swing state like Nevada, the consequences could truly influence the outcome of the presidency. That’s why I’m reaching out today: **[If you believe in fair, secure, and accessible elections, I’m asking you to split a donation between my campaign and the Democratic Association of Secretaries of State. This will be one of the most consequential statewide races in 2026, and your $3 support helps us build the operation we need to win.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122078900/591508655/1273925682?refcode=em-12.27.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTkxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiN2I4N2QzZC01OGUzLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=MX1-4EL8k6IBwmACLgEPv6_q2Et2btfJF6fREjY1EBE=)** __If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: __ | | | **[Chip in $10 Now](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122078901/591508657/1273925682?refcode=em-12.27.25-all-a&express_lane=true&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTkxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiN2I4N2QzZC01OGUzLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=MX1-4EL8k6IBwmACLgEPv6_q2Et2btfJF6fREjY1EBE=)**

name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez
subject: I don’t believe in a ‘fear only’ approach.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
#
We live in a constant state of injustice. We are always struggling against systems of mass incarceration, of extreme income inequality, of late stage capitalism. Those conditions do exist.
But one thing that I think is important is that we must mobilize for an affirmative vision for this country — we can’t just mobilize on fear.
We need to be clear about what the stakes are. We do. **But I don’t believe in a ‘fear only’ approach**.
I’ll tell you what I believe: I believe in a bare minimum.
A $15 minimum wage needed to be passed several years ago. I believe in guaranteed, improved, and expanded Medicare to every single American.
We can’t give up. I’ve been in that place when I was a waitress. I did spend some time giving up and being cynical and saying, “F*ck this.”
And you know what it got us? Nothing. Depression, cynicism, and a sense of purposelessness. Giving up is a privilege that people just can’t afford.
Movements wax and wane. And there are times when it feels like our power is waning, and it’s really easy to take our ball and go home. **But the strength of a movement really depends on the durability of it, and the commitment to it**.
That black pill of cynicism is the death of movements, not the birth of them.
In solidarity,
Alexandria
[Help us continue the fight by making a grassroots donation.](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20251227.vAx4LC&refcode=em20251227-10475-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=10475%2E2526796%2E-obZyS)

name: Marsha Blackburn
subject: Peter, you've been a big part of my team! Will you do me a huge favor?
body:
View my message to you >>>

Peter,

Congress is like a battlefield—thrilling, mission-driven, and full of conflict. At least no one's shooting at us here. Well, not with guns, anyway.

The out-of-touch fake news "reporters" at Politico decided they would target the most dangerous men in DC: **Navy SEALs.**

We all ran for office because we were still hungry to protect and fight for our country. The war may be over for us, but we can't abandon the part of our lives that made us who we are. We were part of an elite unit on the front lines, and Congress is somewhere else we can be out front doing something special for our country.

_**Yet the liberal news corporations, who _desperately_ want to keep the status quo, _attacked us_ for wanting to take on the Swamp establishment and business-as-usual going on in our government.**_

clean_body: They’re targeting the most dangerous men in DC: Navy SEALs. | Peter, Congress is like a battlefield—thrilling, mission-driven, and full of conflict. At least no one's shooting at us here. Well, not with guns, anyway. The out-of-touch fake news "reporters" at Politico decided they would target the most dangerous men in DC: Navy SEALs. We all ran for office because we were still hungry to protect and fight for our country. The war may be over for us, but we can't abandon the part of our lives that made us who we are. We were part of an elite unit on the front lines, and Congress is somewhere else we can be out front doing something special for our country. Yet the liberal news corporations, who desperately want to keep the status quo, attacked us for wanting to take on the Swamp establishment and business-as-usual going on in our government. All of us –Navy SEALs Tim Sheehy, Eli Crane, and Ryan Zinke – are teaming up to stop career politicians and lazy bureaucrats, but we're up against more than just the DC establishment. We're up against the liberal media, RINOs, and revenge-seeking Democrats. We need you, Peter to keep us on the frontlines, bucking the corrupt status quo! -> If the media wants to attack us for having a "warrior mentality" - the mentality to do whatever it takes to achieve a mission... -> If the media wants to attack us for supporting President Trump's MAGA movement - the movement that says we can make America great... -> If the media wants to attack us for taking on the government corruption, fraud, and waste - which has been exposed by Trump's DOGE department... ...Then they don't understand WHY Donald J. body:
Where we’ve been this month.

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122079343/591509707/1239926708?refcode=ea.9784c379-5ae3-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ms=ea.9784c379-5ae3-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5Nzg0YzM3OS01YWUzLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=n6DVfhEHB7mNqzxAbvpAcGCo-C3uBkplR_RCkdpp_mo=&emci=8726a144-a9db-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=9784c379-5ae3-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=51844520) Friends, it’s been a busy December here at Team Phil HQ! Read on for a quick recap of where we’ve been this month.
* * *

name: Haley Stevens
subject: Will you personally endorse my campaign?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate
body:
I'm humbly asking

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=1&akid=1746%2E49239%2EfVOZdP)
null, I wanted to quickly follow up to make sure you got my note.
---
I'm still looking for another 45 grassroots Democrats to personally endorse my campaign to become Michigan's next senator, and**I noticed you haven't had a chance to add your name yet.**
**That's why I'm humbly asking one more time:[Will you personally endorse my campaign to keep Michigan blue, take back the Senate for Democrats, and put a stop to Trump's disastrous agenda?](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10892?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=2&akid=1746%2E49239%2EfVOZdP)**
| | [ADD YOUR NAME](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10892?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=3&akid=1746%2E49239%2EfVOZdP)
---
Thank you for your support. It means the world to have you by my side in this race.

Haley Stevens
\---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: **Haley Stevens**
Subject: What people like you have been telling me
| | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11050?t=4&akid=1746%2E49239%2EfVOZdP)
---
null,
On the campaign trail, I've been talking directly with Michiganders across the state – folks like you who are concerned for the future – and **here's what I've been hearing:**
🛟 People are worried about the future of Medicare and Social Security.

🩺 Folks in Michigan and across the country want improved and affordable access to health care.

💰 Working families need real action to create and protect good-paying jobs.

⛓️💥 And many expressed deep concerns about the future of our fundamental freedoms, civil rights, and American democracy itself.
These aren’t just political talking points, null. They’re _real_ concerns from _real_ people. And they’re exactly why I’m running for Michigan's battleground Senate seat – **because I have a track record of getting things done.**
📌 I helped stabilize the auto industry and save 200,000 jobs as chief of staff on President Obama's Auto Rescue Task Force.
📌 I played a leading role in passing the bill to grow our manufacturing economy by bringing America’s semiconductor industry back to where it belongs: Michigan.
📌 Recently, I introduced the Stop Trump's Abuse of Power Act – a bill to ban the president from deploying active duty troops on U.S. soil without consent from state or local leaders.
That barely scratches the surface, and I know there's a _lot_ more work to do. So it's a good thing I grew up the Michigan way: surrounded by grease, grit, and hard work. And I'm in this race now so I can continue fighting for Michigan's working families in the U.S. Senate.
But with polling showing that I'm the Democrat with the very best shot at beating MAGA Republican Mike Rogers, the GOP is getting scared – **and they're gearing up to spend millions to flip this seat red.**
**So, null, I need****you on my grassroots team to help me****keep up my track record of fighting for hardworking Michigan families.****[Will you join me by endorsing my campaign today? My team set an ambitious goal to get 15,000 signatures by midnight, and right now, I’m still 27% short.](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10892?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=5&akid=1746%2E49239%2EfVOZdP)**
| | [Add Your Name](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10892?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=6&akid=1746%2E49239%2EfVOZdP)
---
Thank you so much for your support,

Haley Stevens
|
* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** Null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you’re in this fight with me, will you add your name to endorse my campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10892?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=7&akid=1746%2E49239%2EfVOZdP) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11050?t=8&akid=1746%2E49239%2EfVOZdP)
---
You can also text “JOIN” to 80242 to become a core member of our team and receive exclusive, real-time updates from my campaign.
Thank you,
Haley
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
[To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=9&akid=1746%2E49239%2EfVOZdP)
Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate.
33717 Woodward Ave #539
Birmingham, MI 48009

name: Team Cooper
subject: Donald Trump raised the stakes of this race
body:
4 days left til our deadline.
|
# [](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2365?source=&t=1&akid=223%2E124072%2E-iRAUB)
| Peter, we need your attention. _This month, the stakes of this race got even higher._
---
Donald Trump PERSONALLY attacked Roy. _He called him a “radical left lunatic.”_
---
Trump’s message is clear: _This Senate seat is his top target. Our campaign is a threat to the GOP._
---
Peter, experts were already expecting this to become the most expensive U.S. Senate race in history. The first to cost a billion dollars or more. The far-right money was already pouring in — but now it’s going to get even worse. _Trump’s name-calling is a signal to the GOP money machine: Take Roy Cooper down._
---
**[Only 4 days remain until the last public filing deadline of 2025, and Roy needs your help. Donate $4 or more to close the gap to our $1 million goal so we can show EVERYONE that the momentum is on our side.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3263?t=7&akid=223%2E124072%2E-iRAUB)** | [DONATE $4](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3263?t=8&akid=223%2E124072%2E-iRAUB)
---
[DONATE MORE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3264?t=9&akid=223%2E124072%2E-iRAUB)
---
* * *
Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here.

National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us.

Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party.

Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy.

**[Donate today to help win this seat and flip the Senate blue.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?source=&t=2&akid=223%2E124072%2E-iRAUB)**
[DONATE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?source=&t=3&akid=223%2E124072%2E-iRAUB)
---

Raleigh, NC 27602
Paid for by Cooper Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Cooper for North Carolina, DSCC, and North Carolina Democratic Party — Federal.
---
Contributions or gifts to Cooper Victory Fund are not tax deductible.
The first $7,000 of each contribution from a person and the first $10,000 from a multicandidate committee (“PAC”) will be allocated to Cooper for North Carolina. The first $3,500 of a person’s contribution and the first $5,000 of a PAC’s contribution will be designated for the primary election; the next $3,500/$5,000 will be designated for the general election. The next $10,000 from a person and $5,000 from a PAC will be allocated to North Carolina Democratic Party – Federal. The next $44,300 of a contribution from a person and the next $15,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC General Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Headquarters Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Legal Fund. A contributor may designate his or her contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if following it would result in an excessive contribution.
Any donor history information in this email reflects what we have on file for this specific email address. If you have donated with a different email address, with a check, or with a spouse — thank you so much! We have that on file and cannot thank you enough for supporting this campaign.

clean_body: 4 days left til our deadline. | | Peter, we need your attention. This month, the stakes of this race got even higher. Donald Trump PERSONALLY attacked Roy. He called him a “radical left lunatic.” Trump’s message is clear: This Senate seat is his top target. Our campaign is a threat to the GOP. Peter, experts were already expecting this to become the most expensive U.S. Senate race in history. The first to cost a billion dollars or more. The far-right money was already pouring in — but now it’s going to get even worse. Trump’s name-calling is a signal to the GOP money machine: Take Roy Cooper down. | Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here. National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us. Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party. Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy. ** mailto:info@roycooper.com | Cooper Victory Fund PO Box 1190 Raleigh, NC 27602 Paid for by Cooper Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Cooper for North Carolina, DSCC, and North Carolina Democratic Party — Federal. Contributions or gifts to Cooper Victory Fund are not tax deductible. The first $7,000 of each contribution from a person and the first $10,000 from a multicandidate committee (“PAC”) will be allocated to Cooper for North Carolina. The first $3,500 of a person’s contribution and the first $5,000 of a PAC’s contribution will be designated for the primary election; the next $3,500/$5,000 will be designated for the general election. The next $10,000 from a person and $5,000 from a PAC will be allocated to North Carolina Democratic Party – Federal. The next $44,300 of a contribution from a person and the next $15,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC General Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Headquarters Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Legal Fund. A contributor may designate his or her contribution for a particular participant. name: JB Pritzker
subject: Something's happening in NH
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Pappas for Senate
body:
It’s Governor JB Pritzker.
I’m emailing you today about New Hampshire’s Senate race because this is serious: **Senate Republicans’ hand-picked candidate, John Sununu, jumped in to defeat Chris Pappas and flip this open Senate seat red.**
**[Before I go any further, I have to ask: Will you rush $5 or more to elect Chris Pappas and keep New Hampshire blue?](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251227_2.8C7438&refcode=em20251227-2&t=1&akid=4124%2E582294%2Evb4es7)**
_Without Chris Pappas winning in New Hampshire, Democrats won’t have a chance at taking back a majority in the Senate._ That’s why this race is being targeted by GOP super PACs and Republicans across the country.
And the NRSC — which pours millions into electing Republicans to the Senate — said they’re “all-in” on John Sununu.
This means New Hampshire will see historic levels of outside spending, and we have to be ready to fight back.
Chris is a proven fighter for working families — and he’s seen tough campaigns like this before. He knows it takes grassroots support to win a race like this. That’s why I’m reaching out to you today.
**[If you’re with me and ready to send Chris Pappas to the Senate, will you chip in $5 or any amount today to help him defend New Hampshire’s seat?](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251227_2.8C7438&refcode=em20251227-2&t=2&akid=4124%2E582294%2Evb4es7)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251227_2.8C7438&amount=5&refcode=em20251227-2&t=21&akid=4124%2E582294%2Evb4es7)**
I know Chris is the type of leader we need in the Senate — **[so thank you for supporting his campaign today.](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/221?c0=em_ms_cp_20251227_2.SsI3K2&refcode=em20251227-2&t=22&akid=4124%2E582294%2Evb4es7)**
With gratitude,
JB Pritzker
Governor of Illinois
[](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/221?c0=em_ms_cp_20251227_2.SsI3K2&refcode=em20251227-2&t=23&akid=4124%2E582294%2Evb4es7)
**New Hampshire is poised to be one of the closest Senate races in the country in 2026. Will you chip in today to help Chris stop the Republicans from flipping the Granite State red and expanding their Senate majority?**
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_cp_footer?refcode=em20251227-2&t=24&refcode2=4124_582294_vb4es7&akid=4124%2E582294%2Evb4es7)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.chrispappas.org/signup/cps_receive_fewer_emails/?t=25&akid=4124%2E582294%2Evb4es7)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.chrispappas.org/signup/cps_update_your_information/?t=26&akid=4124%2E582294%2Evb4es7)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.chrispappas.org/unsubscribe/cps_unsubscribe/?t=27&akid=4124%2E582294%2Evb4es7)

Email us: [teampappas@chrispappas.org](mailto:teampappas@chrispappas.org)
Paid for by Chris Pappas for Senate
Chris Pappas for Senate
PO Box 313
Manchester, NH 03105
United States

Team,

There are nine "Johns" in the Senate right now. There have only been five Black women — **_ever._**

While we're proud Lisa is among the short list of Black women who have been elected to the Senate, we're sad and frankly, disappointed, that — almost _**250 years**_ into our nation’s history — that number is still so low.

**Having people in the Senate who look like the rest of America matters.**

It's how resolutions like Black Women's Equal Pay Day, introduced by Senators Lisa Blunt Rochester and Angela Alsobrooks, get passed. Or how we make sure we advocate for Black maternal healthcare. And it's how we pass legislation honoring the late great John Lewis, expanding voting rights, and protecting people of color.

[**We need more women of color in the places where important decisions are being made. That's why we've set a goal to raise $15,000 to help elect more Black women!**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122079590/591510507/-486912179?refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.27_EBW-Dec-MM_B1_F1_S1_C1_U1_RS-T1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMDllYmE4Zi01Y2UzLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=x_92btL6am6fTSc_v-rdpZh1coLQDF4C1NXYrqjZWUw=)

**But here's a hard truth: Black women raise 1/3 of the funds their white counterparts will. That already puts us at a disadvantage we can't afford.**

If you would like to get less email, To unsubscribe, | | = Team, There are nine "Johns" in the Senate right now. There have only been five Black women — ever. While we're proud Lisa is among the short list of Black women who have been elected to the Senate, we're sad and frankly, disappointed, that — almost 250 years into our nation’s history — that number is still so low. Having people in the Senate who look like the rest of America matters. It's how resolutions like Black Women's Equal Pay Day, introduced by Senators Lisa Blunt Rochester and Angela Alsobrooks, get passed. Or how we make sure we advocate for Black maternal healthcare. And it's how we pass legislation honoring the late great John Lewis, expanding voting rights, and protecting people of color. = But here's a hard truth: Black women raise 1/3 of the funds their white counterparts will. name: Carmela Conroy
subject: We’ve had enough
body:
| | | | | [|
---](https://conroy4congress.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=dc047fbc65030d4c4bcc44d19&id=81cd8d714c&e=a7b299e420)
| | | | | |
Peter,

**As the holiday season comes to a close, our neighbors are feeling the squeeze – meanwhile, Michael Baumgartner is** _**still**_**nowhere to be found.

** As folks worry about winter heating costs, rising food prices, and whether local clinics will stay open, Baumgartner has spent most of the past six months on paid vacation.

And when he does show up to work, he votes lockstep with extreme House leadership – extending the tariff chaos, raising costs, threatening healthcare, and leaving our communities behind.**

We have to hold him accountable, Peter – and make sure his freshman term is his last.

That’s why it’s absolutely critical we hit our final fundraising goal of the year and head into 2026 with the resources we need to win. **[**To stay on track, we set an ambitious goal of raising $28,500 before the clock strikes midnight on New Year's Eve. Will you rush a grassroots contribution to help us close the gap?**](https://conroy4congress.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=dc047fbc65030d4c4bcc44d19&id=3c579d8459&e=a7b299e420)

| | Right now, clinics from Stevens County to Columbia County are warning they may have to cut services or close altogether. Vacant doctor, nurse, and counselor positions at Veterans’ hospitals and clinics have been slashed, straining remaining staff and creating longer wait times. Families are forced to choose between paying skyrocketing insurance premiums or putting food on the table.

**This is a crisis of leadership.

**Baumgartner abandoned Eastern Washington the moment he got to Congress. We deserve better. I’m ready to go to Congress and fight for you, your family, friends, and neighbors – every single day.
[**Will you pitch in now to help us continue building the campaign we need to win?**](https://conroy4congress.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=dc047fbc65030d4c4bcc44d19&id=2f8e622edd&e=a7b299e420)**

** Thank you for stepping up - and for being a part of Team Eastern Washington.

Carmela Conroy

The only way we'll stop Donald Trump is by diluting his political power everywhere it exists — in governors' mansions, in state legislatures, in Congress, and in every office where decisions about our rights and freedoms are made. clean_body: | Peter, The only way we'll stop Donald Trump is by diluting his political power everywhere it exists — in governors' mansions, in state legislatures, in Congress, and in every office where decisions about our rights and freedoms are made. And that means electing Democratic pro-choice women up and down the ballot. | When I get scared or angry or discouraged about the state of our politics, I think about the women you've helped elect already. Our governors are protecting fundamental freedoms as Trump and his allies try to rip them away. Our secretaries of state are defending the very foundation of our democracy. Our attorneys general are safeguarding the rule of law and pushing back against Trump's agenda every single day. So many of these women ran in tough races. But this community believed in them, fought for them, and gave them the support they needed to win. And now they're leading the charge against Republican chaos. As we head toward the end of this year, Trump is doing everything he can to force his agenda onto this country, and we need to be ready the moment the calendar turns to fuel our candidates up and down the ballot to victory. That's why we’re launching our End-of-Year Fund today. This fund will determine how strong our first steps into the new year can be. It fuels early investments, candidate training, and the groundwork our women need to compete and win in the toughest races. ************ If you've saved your payment information with FastAction, your donation will go through immediately: | ---|--- | | Thank you, Jessica Mackler President, EMILYs List = | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122080526/591514189/-272948131?refcode=AU_MTH_FR_GEN_EOQ_AC_20251227_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwYzYzYjU4MC01ZmUzLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xO-jotWJrFClxS6NeESrcPjfXdJhPrzIQo8LRn3FkG0=&emci=22f5ee05-03dd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&emdi=0c63b580-5fe3-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=252045)

Folks, I hope you are getting a chance to enjoy the holiday season. I don’t want to take up too much of your time, but this is important: We are just days away from the end of the year — and the end of the fundraising quarter. Federal campaigns are required to submit fundraising numbers to the Federal Election Commission. These reports show how much money we spent and how much money we raised. When our FEC report is released, Republicans will comb through every page for signs of weakness.**Reaching our goal will send a powerful message that everyday people believe in our shared New Mexico values and want to elect more Democratic leaders. ** Folks, we need to hit the ground running in 2026 to help elect more Democrats to the U.S. Senate. And I’ll be honest with you, the Senate map is stacked against Democrats next year. It will be hard for Democrats to flip enough seats to win the majority in the Senate — but not impossible. **What we raise now will determine our ability to organize, mobilize, and turnout voters ahead of the critical 2026 elections. That’s why I’m humbly asking:** [**Will you help us meet our end-of-year deadline today to help elect more Democrats and build a future where all New Mexicans and Americans can thrive?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122080527/591514190/944546612?refcode=AU_MTH_FR_GEN_EOQ_AC_20251227_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwYzYzYjU4MC01ZmUzLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xO-jotWJrFClxS6NeESrcPjfXdJhPrzIQo8LRn3FkG0=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [CHIP IN $5 NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122080528/591514191/944546612?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=AU_MTH_FR_GEN_EOQ_AC_20251227_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwYzYzYjU4MC01ZmUzLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xO-jotWJrFClxS6NeESrcPjfXdJhPrzIQo8LRn3FkG0=)

name: Jocelyn Benson
subject: I’m personally asking
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOCELYN BENSON FOR GOVERNOR
body:
# [](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/203?t=1&akid=1186%2E181023%2ED36DRT)
Friend — Our campaign to make Michigan the best place to call home grew so much over this past year.
Thanks to supporters like you, I’ve been able to spend the year traveling across Michigan, meeting with voters to discuss the future of our state and how we can make it an affordable place for anyone to thrive.
**Here’s what I’ve heard: They want a governor who will fight every single day to ensure that their families have a fair shot.**
I am running to be that governor, to bring transparent leadership, safe schools and neighborhoods, and world-class education and childcare to Michigan.
_But I can’t do it without your help._
**Our end-of-the-year fundraising goal expires in a few days, and what we’re able to raise before then will determine the strength of our campaign.**
**[That’s why I’m personally asking: Can you chip in a donation before our fundraising deadline on Wednesday to help us reach our goal and continue growing this campaign?](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/239?c0=jmb-em-q42025.hprbVa&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_EOQ_AC_20251227_PM1_V1_S1&t=2&akid=1186%2E181023%2ED36DRT)**

name: Mark Kelly
subject: I will not give up on our country.
body:
It has given me too much, and I’ll never be able to repay that debt. I still believe in our democracy, our values, and our Constitution.
Peter,
If you can believe it, things didn’t stop when Trump called for my hanging. It didn’t stop with the threat of a court-martial from Pete Hegseth, either. It didn’t stop when the FBI wanted to talk to me and other members of Congress. Because now the Department of Defense has announced that it is escalating its investigation into me.
All for saying things the president didn’t like to hear.
If you’re feeling like this isn’t the country you grew up in, I don’t blame you. But I hope you aren’t giving up on it, Peter.
I certainly haven’t. This country has given me too much, and I’ll never be able to repay that debt. I still believe in our democracy, our values, and our Constitution.
There’s no chance in hell that somebody like Donald Trump is going to stop me from serving the people of this nation. None.
If he thought all of this was going to shut me up, he’s got another thing coming.
And if you’d be willing to help support me, please:
**[Split a donation between Wes Moore and me. It would mean a lot.](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251227-2.Ert_Qo&refcode=em-20251227-2&t=1&akid=1116%2E1086043%2EmGXXpi)**
**[Donate $5 ››](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251227-2.Ert_Qo&amount=5&refcode=em-20251227-2&t=20&akid=1116%2E1086043%2EmGXXpi)**
Thank you,
Mark
[](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251227-2.Ert_Qo&refcode=em-20251227-2&t=21&akid=1116%2E1086043%2EmGXXpi)

subject: Another child is still waiting.
body:
Gabriela’s story is proof of what your generosity makes possible. But so many children are still waiting.

| [View in Web Browser](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e10193659/VaHR0cHM6Ly93cm8ubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3YvRlJRRlQ1UmtwWQ/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEyMjc/LbmNscExCU2dtbw/qP2hpZHM9bmNaMU50UHpXaVFLeDlscw/gaVA_-A/s9r064edcb9)
| | [](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e10193659/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYW5lZG90LmNvbS9zb3VuZC1vZi1mcmVlZG9tLWZvdW5kYXRpb24tMmExZDVlODctOGRhNy00N2ZmLTlkODUtZTJlYzEyYTZmZjE4LzJkMzk3MjQ0LWZmOTYtNGY3Mi04OGNmLTE4NmFjMjQ3MWMyYw/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEyMjc/LbmNscExCU2dtbw/qP3NvdXJjZV9jb2RlPVdBUlI3OTA5OQ/gaVA_-A/sama3588da0)

Patriot,

Gabriela was only 9 years old, trapped in a small apartment, her childhood stolen by traffickers. The curtains were drawn, the doors were locked, and she was told no one would come for her.

But she was wrong. Because of people like you, Gabriela was rescued.

**Rescues like Gabriela's only happen because of support from donors like you. Generosity is the reason Gabriela’s story ends in freedom, not tragedy.**

But for every Gabriela, there are countless other children still waiting in the shadows, hoping someone will come.

**[Will you give $25, $50, or even $125 today to make sure another child’s story ends in freedom instead of captivity?](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e10193659/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYW5lZG90LmNvbS9zb3VuZC1vZi1mcmVlZG9tLWZvdW5kYXRpb24tMmExZDVlODctOGRhNy00N2ZmLTlkODUtZTJlYzEyYTZmZjE4LzJkMzk3MjQ0LWZmOTYtNGY3Mi04OGNmLTE4NmFjMjQ3MWMyYw/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEyMjc/LbmNscExCU2dtbw/qP3NvdXJjZV9jb2RlPVdBUlI3OTA5OQ/gaVA_-A/sama3588da0)**
---
[](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e10193659/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYW5lZG90LmNvbS9zb3VuZC1vZi1mcmVlZG9tLWZvdW5kYXRpb24tMmExZDVlODctOGRhNy00N2ZmLTlkODUtZTJlYzEyYTZmZjE4LzJkMzk3MjQ0LWZmOTYtNGY3Mi04OGNmLTE4NmFjMjQ3MWMyYw/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEyMjc/LbmNscExCU2dtbw/qP3NvdXJjZV9jb2RlPVdBUlI3OTA5OQ/gaVA_-A/sama3588da0)
---
[ **Be the Reason a Child is Rescued** ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e10193659/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYW5lZG90LmNvbS9zb3VuZC1vZi1mcmVlZG9tLWZvdW5kYXRpb24tMmExZDVlODctOGRhNy00N2ZmLTlkODUtZTJlYzEyYTZmZjE4LzJkMzk3MjQ0LWZmOTYtNGY3Mi04OGNmLTE4NmFjMjQ3MWMyYw/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEyMjc/LbmNscExCU2dtbw/qP3NvdXJjZV9jb2RlPVdBUlI3OTA5OQ/gaVA_-A/sama3588da0)
---
Every gift to the Sound of Freedom Foundation fuels this mission in real, concrete ways that immediately make a difference: **You fund prevention.**
Your support strengthens vulnerable families, making sure traffickers cannot exploit poverty or desperation. Every meal, every safe home, every act of care is a shield around a child. **You fund intervention.**
Your generosity equips our partners on the ground — those who respond when a tip comes in, those who risk everything to open the locked doors where children are trapped. Gabriela’s rescue happened because intervention teams, supported by donors like you, were ready to act. **You fund survivor care.**
When children like Gabriela are pulled from captivity, they need so much more than a safe bed. They need medical attention, counseling, education, and steady love. Your gift surrounds them with that care so they can learn to smile and trust again. **You fund education.**
Many victims are hidden in plain sight, in schools, in clinics, in neighborhoods where no one knows what to look for. Your gift spreads awareness and equips first responders to see the signs and act decisively. **You fund influence.**
Your generosity gives us the ability to advocate with lawmakers and leaders across the world. It’s how we keep pressure on governments to act, to change laws, and to dismantle the systems that let traffickers thrive. **When you make your gift to Sound of Freedom Foundation, you are changing the ending for children who would otherwise be forgotten.** [**Will you give $25, $50, $125, or whatever you can right now to rescue the next child?**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e10193659/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYW5lZG90LmNvbS9zb3VuZC1vZi1mcmVlZG9tLWZvdW5kYXRpb24tMmExZDVlODctOGRhNy00N2ZmLTlkODUtZTJlYzEyYTZmZjE4LzJkMzk3MjQ0LWZmOTYtNGY3Mi04OGNmLTE4NmFjMjQ3MWMyYw/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEyMjc/LbmNscExCU2dtbw/qP3NvdXJjZV9jb2RlPVdBUlI3OTA5OQ/gaVA_-A/sama3588da0)
---
[ **Yes — I Will Help Rescue a Child** ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e10193659/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYW5lZG90LmNvbS9zb3VuZC1vZi1mcmVlZG9tLWZvdW5kYXRpb24tMmExZDVlODctOGRhNy00N2ZmLTlkODUtZTJlYzEyYTZmZjE4LzJkMzk3MjQ0LWZmOTYtNGY3Mi04OGNmLTE4NmFjMjQ3MWMyYw/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEyMjc/LbmNscExCU2dtbw/qP3NvdXJjZV9jb2RlPVdBUlI3OTA5OQ/gaVA_-A/sama3588da0)
---
The hard truth is this: while Gabriela is safe tonight, another child is still staring at a locked door. Another little boy is whispering prayers into the dark, hoping someone hears him. Another girl is being told the same lie Gabriela heard, that no one will ever come. **Patriot, I _refuse_ to let that be the end of their story. I am a fighter for every single one of God’s children. But it takes more than my passion, it takes us working together.** **[Will you give $25, $50, or $125 today to slam the door on traffickers and throw open the door of freedom for the next child?](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e10193659/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYW5lZG90LmNvbS9zb3VuZC1vZi1mcmVlZG9tLWZvdW5kYXRpb24tMmExZDVlODctOGRhNy00N2ZmLTlkODUtZTJlYzEyYTZmZjE4LzJkMzk3MjQ0LWZmOTYtNGY3Mi04OGNmLTE4NmFjMjQ3MWMyYw/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEyMjc/LbmNscExCU2dtbw/qP3NvdXJjZV9jb2RlPVdBUlI3OTA5OQ/gaVA_-A/sama3588da0)**
---
[ **Rescue Cannot Wait — Give Now** ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e10193659/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYW5lZG90LmNvbS9zb3VuZC1vZi1mcmVlZG9tLWZvdW5kYXRpb24tMmExZDVlODctOGRhNy00N2ZmLTlkODUtZTJlYzEyYTZmZjE4LzJkMzk3MjQ0LWZmOTYtNGY3Mi04OGNmLTE4NmFjMjQ3MWMyYw/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEyMjc/LbmNscExCU2dtbw/qP3NvdXJjZV9jb2RlPVdBUlI3OTA5OQ/gaVA_-A/sama3588da0)
---
Imagine every locked door opened. Every frightened child rescued. Every trafficker facing true justice. That is the future we are building together. And it only happens because people like you refuse to quit until every child is safe. **[Will you give your most generous gift today and help write the next chapter of freedom for a child?](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e10193659/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYW5lZG90LmNvbS9zb3VuZC1vZi1mcmVlZG9tLWZvdW5kYXRpb24tMmExZDVlODctOGRhNy00N2ZmLTlkODUtZTJlYzEyYTZmZjE4LzJkMzk3MjQ0LWZmOTYtNGY3Mi04OGNmLTE4NmFjMjQ3MWMyYw/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEyMjc/LbmNscExCU2dtbw/qP3NvdXJjZV9jb2RlPVdBUlI3OTA5OQ/gaVA_-A/sama3588da0)**
---
[ **Be the Hero — Rescue a Child Today** ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e10193659/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYW5lZG90LmNvbS9zb3VuZC1vZi1mcmVlZG9tLWZvdW5kYXRpb24tMmExZDVlODctOGRhNy00N2ZmLTlkODUtZTJlYzEyYTZmZjE4LzJkMzk3MjQ0LWZmOTYtNGY3Mi04OGNmLTE4NmFjMjQ3MWMyYw/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEyMjc/LbmNscExCU2dtbw/qP3NvdXJjZV9jb2RlPVdBUlI3OTA5OQ/gaVA_-A/sama3588da0)

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/fcd44d17-4aa4-45b7-aad3-b999bca791c8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/fcd44d17-4aa4-45b7-aad3-b999bca791c8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
_**By Ellie Leonard, Blue Amp Media Contributing Editor**_
When I wrote the article "_[How Jeffrey Epstein Cosplayed His Way Into Harvard](https://substack.com/redirect/cedc6f35-ba44-4572-ba07-0fa923f04422?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_ " last July, I knew it was unfinished. There were too many stories about chance meetings, unqualified positions in higher academia, and unfathomable leaps in income that, on paper, still don't make sense.
How does a guy with no college degree get a job teaching math and physics at a K-12 private school that today costs $67,840/year? How does he bounce onto Wall Street and make millions, then end up with an office and a key card at Harvard?
I knew [there was more to the story, more money, and certainly more victims](https://substack.com/redirect/2d8eaa46-b206-4903-b02d-ae4d19888e63?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), as students recounted seeing Epstein at their parties, the only teacher who ever went.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5b6709c7-f01b-4ea2-a621-44b82591a40d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Alchetron.com
* * *
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=182656854&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgyNjU2ODU0LCJpYXQiOjE3NjY4NzA5MDUsImV4cCI6MTc2OTQ2MjkwNSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.8oZSJOSopn_Nuaptv7xvp6PJKwHSz1h4apSGvfq7M4A)
* * *
Epstein made his way into Harvard's good graces with a $200,000 donation to the school's psych department, led by chair Stephen Kosselyn. He'd make another $6.5 million payment to Marvin Nowak, head of the Program for Evolutionary Dynamics, a research program (i.e. not an actual department), in 2003. This led to said key card and "Jeffrey's Office," and he was appointed a visiting fellowship two years later.
Upon his arrest in 2006 for sexual trafficking of a minor (which led to his 2008 "sweetheart deal"), Epstein withdrew his application for the 2006-2007 school year. However, he visited Harvard often and kept his social position on campus, including his office, until 2018.
In July, I interviewed a student from the Program for Evolutionary Dynamics, who graduated in 2005. The following quotes are from that interview.
> #### "Nowak is a renowned evolutionary biologist," the student said, "he was wooed to Harvard from the Institute of Advanced Study at Princeton--he'd been at Oxford before that--with the promise that he'd get to run this Program for Evolutionary Dynamics that was being founded on the basis of an Epstein donation."
Epstein had planned to give much more.
> #### "Word on the street then was that $30 million was promised. The rest of the money never materialized, which led to some problems down the line, and eventually resulted in the program being shuttered in disgrace."
He talks about Epstein's other-worldly presence, drawing in students, and more often professors, who viewed him as a kind of legend on campus, fawning on him every chance they got, both for his money and sheer proximity.
> #### "Due to Epstein's largess, the program was housed in a fancy office space on the top of a building in Brattle Square, had lovely views, excellent free coffee, lots of reasons to go there and just hang out. [Epstein] occasionally made 'state visits,' as it were, and usually when he came there would be a to-do. [Celebrities would gather to greet him. This was very much part and parcel of the culture](https://substack.com/redirect/debff9f0-4f9a-4141-afe8-67c48fb41e35?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) at Harvard at the time."
The students surmised that Epstein wouldn't have received the treatment he got, had it not been for the donations, but noted that Epstein seemed more interested in the science than most donors. The professors felt flattered, he said, as if they, too, held some high-level status when he was around.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2a6294d2-13af-4cfa-9320-2e59bc1c6462?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Getty Images
* * *
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/fcd44d17-4aa4-45b7-aad3-b999bca791c8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
When asked why someone without a college degree could receive a fellowship at an Ivy League school, the student downplayed the notion.
> #### "I suppose the context in which these sort of things happen is that there has been, with the years, increasing professionalization - or l[et's say 'corporatization' seeping deeper into every nook and cranny](https://substack.com/redirect/84d03094-f049-430d-80d8-d35363f171f1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of the university, with many arrangements that used to be ubiquitous and informal becoming increasingly formalized over the years. So Kosselyn arranged that fellowship." The student shrugs. "So yeah, it's nepotism or quid-pro-quo, but those things weren't considered a big deal because they weren't."
I ask him about Harvard's response to the first scandal in 2006.
> #### "They pretend now that they put their foot down very hard and said, 'no, no,' and were, you know, all righteousness and probity. But I think the prevailing attitude was, 'Yeah, you can't [receive donations] directly anymore; it would look bad,' but it was not an excommunication. I would think with a nod and a wink, the professors who'd been the beneficiaries of his largesse in the past, were encouraged, and certainly not discouraged, from maintaining some level of ties with Epstein."
At some point Harvard began taking indirect payments as a way to distance themselves from Epstein, but maintain funding.
> #### "So Leon Black ended up giving money for the [Program of Evolutionary Dynamics], and when asked, denied that he took the money from Epstein to give."
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e3c1d82f-b7b5-4f1d-81eb-9516ee6b3ef9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
From the investigation by Harvard
_Leon Black, co-founder of the private equity firm Apollo Global Management, would later be flagged by JPMorgan as one of four main actors (also Alan Dershowitz, Glenn Dubin, and Leslie Wexner) participating in nearly 4700 transactions that totaled more than $1 billion. This also included transfers to Russian banks and "sensitivities around his relationships with two U.S. presidents," [Donald Trump](https://substack.com/redirect/e9083315-1655-422b-9e92-88b965024bd2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and Bill Clinton._
* * *
We're growing Blue Amp Media with breakthrough reporters like Ellie Leonard. Sign up for free!
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.Ytcf_1Y459SC5_tzgGOmNmRYj6Es-8FfZFESnCXXxWw?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=182656854)
* * *
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/0a166319-1047-4c2f-81f1-a33c7b7e602a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Jeffrey Epstein and Alan Dershowitz - Getty Images
The student says that in 2016 Epstein also made private payments to Elisa New, a professor of poetry at Harvard, who just so happened to be Larry Summers' girlfriend. And Larry Summers just so happened to be the former President of Harvard. Both New and Summers flew to Epstein's island, Little Saint James, in 2005.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/45cf368b-3ef7-49a2-be26-5f61b1d9ae46?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Ultimately the student stands up for Martin Nowak, the head of his department.
> #### "Harvard ended up making Martin Nowak the scapegoat for all of this, and tried very, very hard to fire him, and ultimately failed to find grounds to fire him, but has continued making his life miserable."
The student found no fault in Nowak, claiming that he simply was in the wrong place at the wrong time.
> #### "[It's] a damn shame. He is an excellent scientist and was overall a nice guy."
The student remembers that Epstein had a reputation among grad students in the Program for Evolutionary Dynamics, and that he was "well-known to have an eye for the ladies." He says Epstein often brought along a young female companion "probably around my age at the time," who looked like a model. "And though she was introduced as Epstein's 'assistant,' her role seemed to be entirely decorative." Epstein didn't often invite students to his soirees, but he did remember one.
> #### "There was a very up-and-coming female graduate student, also very good-looking, who got an invitation to join some professors who were all being invited to Epstein's ranch in New Mexico."
I finished the interview, put away my notes, and honestly didn't think about the things the student said for many months, hoping I might pull from it at some point if I needed an academic perspective.
_Then Virginia Giuffre 's book came out._
> #### "One thing that was happening, and with increasing regularity, was that I was being sexually trafficked by Epstein and Maxwell. The second person I was lent out to was a psychology professor whose research Epstein was helping to fund." \- Virginia Giuffre
_Epstein made his way into Harvard 's good graces with a $200,000 donation to the school's psych department, led by chair Stephen Kosselyn. Virginia Giuffre does not expressly mention Kosselyn's name, and so he cannot directly be linked to the above statement._
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/57d74492-eaea-423b-9466-5592978cc57c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Jeffrey Epstein and Marvin Minsky - Getty Images
She was also trafficked to computer scientist and MIT professor Marvin Minsky, known as the "father of AI." He was in his 70s at the time; Virginia was 17.
> #### "I will never forget Minsky's bald head, and the way his face seemed to have shriveled like one of those folk-art dolls whose heads are dried-up apples. Throughout my time having sex with Minsky, I could hear the waves lapping outside the little room. I tried to focus only on that sound." \- Virginia Giuffre
_Jeffrey Epstein paid $850,000 to support Minsky 's work at MIT and fund the MIT Media Lab. Only $100,000 came before his 2008 conviction for procuring a minor for prostitution. The other $750,000 came after, continuing until 2017._
The Program for Evolutionary Dynamics announced its closure in 2021, following an investigation into Martin Nowak's relationship with Jeffrey Epstein after 2008, which included cited policy violations, negligence, and unprofessional conduct. Vicky Ward's 2003 article "_The Talented Mr. Epstein_ ," gave evidence of his unabashed involvement with the disgraced financier.
> #### "Nowak says that, when he goes to stay with Epstein in the Caribbean, they'll get up at six and, as the sun rises, have three-hour conversations about theoretical physics. 'Then he'll go off and do some work, re-appear, and we'll talk some more.'" \- Vicky Ward, _Vanity Fair_
_At the time Epstein had pledged $25 million to Harvard to create the "Epstein Program for Mathematical Biology and Evolutionary Dynamics."_
In 2011, Nowak would write a book, _SuperCooperators,_ in which he described Little St. James as "a luxury retreat with patina-finished roofs, and a mile-long beach dotted with palm trees." This was three years after Epstein's first conviction for sexually abusing a minor.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ad02db71-4ffa-4568-99cc-69f16e25a7a4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Martin Nowak - The Chronicle of Higher Education
Martin Nowak is still working as a professor of mathematics and biology at Harvard University.
Former psychology chair at Harvard, Stephen Kosselyn, is now the President and CEO of Active Learning Sciences, Inc., a AI-based company that creates educational programs.
Marvin Minsky died in 2016, three years before Jeffrey Epstein would be arrested at Teterboro Airport.
* * *
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/fcd44d17-4aa4-45b7-aad3-b999bca791c8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
You're currently a free subscriber to [Blue Amp Media](https://substack.com/redirect/860d3037-87ca-49bc-940f-e2698098ea3e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Inside the donor culture that traded access, silence, and credibility for money | | and | Dec 27 | | | ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- | | ---|---|--- By Ellie Leonard, Blue Amp Media Contributing Editor When I wrote the article " " last July, I knew it was unfinished. There were too many stories about chance meetings, unqualified positions in higher academia, and unfathomable leaps in income that, on paper, still don't make sense. How does a guy with no college degree get a job teaching math and physics at a K-12 private school that today costs $67,840/year? How does he bounce onto Wall Street and make millions, then end up with an office and a key card at Harvard? I knew , as students recounted seeing Epstein at their parties, the only teacher who ever went. | | ---|---|--- Alchetron.com Epstein made his way into Harvard's good graces with a $200,000 donation to the school's psych department, led by chair Stephen Kosselyn. He'd make another $6.5 million payment to Marvin Nowak, head of the Program for Evolutionary Dynamics, a research program (i.e. not an actual department), in 2003. This led to said key card and "Jeffrey's Office," and he was appointed a visiting fellowship two years later. Upon his arrest in 2006 for sexual trafficking of a minor (which led to his 2008 "sweetheart deal"), Epstein withdrew his application for the 2006-2007 school year. However, he visited Harvard often and kept his social position on campus, including his office, until 2018. In July, I interviewed a student from the Program for Evolutionary Dynamics, who graduated in 2005. The following quotes are from that interview. #### "Nowak is a renowned evolutionary biologist," the student said, "he was wooed to Harvard from the Institute of Advanced Study at Princeton--he'd been at Oxford before that--with the promise that he'd get to run this Program for Evolutionary Dynamics that was being founded on the basis of an Epstein donation." Epstein had planned to give much more. #### "Word on the street then was that $30 million was promised. The rest of the money never materialized, which led to some problems down the line, and eventually resulted in the program being shuttered in disgrace." He talks about Epstein's other-worldly presence, drawing in students, and more often professors, who viewed him as a kind of legend on campus, fawning on him every chance they got, both for his money and sheer proximity. #### "Due to Epstein's largess, the program was housed in a fancy office space on the top of a building in Brattle Square, had lovely views, excellent free coffee, lots of reasons to go there and just hang out. [Epstein] occasionally made 'state visits,' as it were, and usually when he came there would be a to-do. at Harvard at the time." The students surmised that Epstein wouldn't have received the treatment he got, had it not been for the donations, but noted that Epstein seemed more interested in the science than most donors. The professors felt flattered, he said, as if they, too, held some high-level status when he was around. | | ---|---|--- Getty Images | | ---|---|--- When asked why someone without a college degree could receive a fellowship at an Ivy League school, the student downplayed the notion. #### "I suppose the context in which these sort of things happen is that there has been, with the years, increasing professionalization - or l of the university, with many arrangements that used to be ubiquitous and informal becoming increasingly formalized over the years. So Kosselyn arranged that fellowship." The student shrugs. "So yeah, it's nepotism or quid-pro-quo, but those things weren't considered a big deal because they weren't." I ask him about Harvard's response to the first scandal in 2006. #### "They pretend now that they put their foot down very hard and said, 'no, no,' and were, you know, all righteousness and probity. But I think the prevailing attitude was, 'Yeah, you can't [receive donations] directly anymore; it would look bad,' but it was not an excommunication. I would think with a nod and a wink, the professors who'd been the beneficiaries of his largesse in the past, were encouraged, and certainly not discouraged, from maintaining some level of ties with Epstein." At some point Harvard began taking indirect payments as a way to distance themselves from Epstein, but maintain funding. #### "So Leon Black ended up giving money for the [Program of Evolutionary Dynamics], and when asked, denied that he took the money from Epstein to give." | | ---|---|--- From the investigation by Harvard Leon Black, co-founder of the private equity firm Apollo Global Management, would later be flagged by JPMorgan as one of four main actors (also Alan Dershowitz, Glenn Dubin, and Leslie Wexner) participating in nearly 4700 transactions that totaled more than $1 billion. This also included transfers to Russian banks and "sensitivities around his relationships with two U.S. presidents," and Bill Clinton. We're growing Blue Amp Media with breakthrough reporters like Ellie Leonard. Sign up for free! | | ---|---|--- Jeffrey Epstein and Alan Dershowitz - Getty Images The student says that in 2016 Epstein also made private payments to Elisa New, a professor of poetry at Harvard, who just so happened to be Larry Summers' girlfriend. And Larry Summers just so happened to be the former President of Harvard. Both New and Summers flew to Epstein's island, Little Saint James, in 2005. | | ---|---|--- Ultimately the student stands up for Martin Nowak, the head of his department. #### "Harvard ended up making Martin Nowak the scapegoat for all of this, and tried very, very hard to fire him, and ultimately failed to find grounds to fire him, but has continued making his life miserable." The student found no fault in Nowak, claiming that he simply was in the wrong place at the wrong time. #### "[It's] a damn shame. He is an excellent scientist and was overall a nice guy." The student remembers that Epstein had a reputation among grad students in the Program for Evolutionary Dynamics, and that he was "well-known to have an eye for the ladies." He says Epstein often brought along a young female companion "probably around my age at the time," who looked like a model. "And though she was introduced as Epstein's 'assistant,' her role seemed to be entirely decorative." Epstein didn't often invite students to his soirees, but he did remember one. #### "There was a very up-and-coming female graduate student, also very good-looking, who got an invitation to join some professors who were all being invited to Epstein's ranch in New Mexico." I finished the interview, put away my notes, and honestly didn't think about the things the student said for many months, hoping I might pull from it at some point if I needed an academic perspective. Then Virginia Giuffre 's book came out. #### "One thing that was happening, and with increasing regularity, was that I was being sexually trafficked by Epstein and Maxwell. The second person I was lent out to was a psychology professor whose research Epstein was helping to fund." \- Virginia Giuffre Epstein made his way into Harvard 's good graces with a $200,000 donation to the school's psych department, led by chair Stephen Kosselyn. Virginia Giuffre does not expressly mention Kosselyn's name, and so he cannot directly be linked to the above statement. | | ---|---|--- Jeffrey Epstein and Marvin Minsky - Getty Images She was also trafficked to computer scientist and MIT professor Marvin Minsky, known as the "father of AI." He was in his 70s at the time; Virginia was 17. #### "I will never forget Minsky's bald head, and the way his face seemed to have shriveled like one of those folk-art dolls whose heads are dried-up apples. Throughout my time having sex with Minsky, I could hear the waves lapping outside the little room. I tried to focus only on that sound." \- Virginia Giuffre Jeffrey Epstein paid $850,000 to support Minsky 's work at MIT and fund the MIT Media Lab. Only $100,000 came before his 2008 conviction for procuring a minor for prostitution. The other $750,000 came after, continuing until 2017. The Program for Evolutionary Dynamics announced its closure in 2021, following an investigation into Martin Nowak's relationship with Jeffrey Epstein after 2008, which included cited policy violations, negligence, and unprofessional conduct. Vicky Ward's 2003 article "The Talented Mr. Epstein ," gave evidence of his unabashed involvement with the disgraced financier. #### "Nowak says that, when he goes to stay with Epstein in the Caribbean, they'll get up at six and, as the sun rises, have three-hour conversations about theoretical physics. 'Then he'll go off and do some work, re-appear, and we'll talk some more.'" \- Vicky Ward, Vanity Fair At the time Epstein had pledged $25 million to Harvard to create the "Epstein Program for Mathematical Biology and Evolutionary Dynamics." In 2011, Nowak would write a book, SuperCooperators, in which he described Little St. James as "a luxury retreat with patina-finished roofs, and a mile-long beach dotted with palm trees." This was three years after Epstein's first conviction for sexually abusing a minor. | | ---|---|--- Martin Nowak - The Chronicle of Higher Education Martin Nowak is still working as a professor of mathematics and biology at Harvard University. Former psychology chair at Harvard, Stephen Kosselyn, is now the President and CEO of Active Learning Sciences, Inc., a AI-based company that creates educational programs. Marvin Minsky died in 2016, three years before Jeffrey Epstein would be arrested at Teterboro Airport. | | ---|---|--- * You're currently a free subscriber to . name: Tammy Baldwin
subject: This race won’t be won on values alone

body:
# [](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/18?refcode=em20251227-948&t=1&akid=948%2E294397%2ER7APPY)
[](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/31?c0=em_ms_jct_20251227_2_ak.55amQT&amount=10&refcode=em20251227-948&t=3&akid=948%2E294397%2ER7APPY)
Hi there,
I’m reaching out today because on December 31, Judge Chris Taylor is facing a _major_ public fundraising deadline.
With her general election just four months away in April, we can’t afford to leave anything to chance.
[**Before I say more, will you join me in supporting Judge Chris Taylor by chipping in $10 or any amount today?**](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/31?c0=em_ms_jct_20251227_2_ak.55amQT&amount=10&refcode=em20251227-948&t=4&akid=948%2E294397%2ER7APPY)
You’ve seen how competitive these races are.
This past summer, the Wisconsin Supreme Court majority was nearly flipped by right-wing special interests and billionaires like Elon Musk trying to buy a seat on the bench.
Now, they’re eyeing up Judge Chris Taylor’s race, and we’re expecting them to spend _big_ once again to try and install someone who will put their extreme agenda ahead of our rights.
That’s why I’m all in for Chris.
From her years as a public servant to her work on the bench, she’s spent her whole career standing up for people and protecting our freedoms. She’s fair. She’s tough. And she’s exactly the kind of justice we need on the Supreme Court.
**But this race won’t be won on values alone. It’s going to take a strong, people-powered movement to fight back — and that starts with showing strength before this deadline.**
[**Can you chip in $10 to help Judge Chris Taylor hit her $115,000 online goal before December 31?**](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/31?c0=em_ms_jct_20251227_2_ak.55amQT&amount=10&refcode=em20251227-948&t=5&akid=948%2E294397%2ER7APPY)
**[Donate $10 ››](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/31?c0=em_ms_jct_20251227_2_ak.55amQT&amount=10&refcode=em20251227-948&t=24&akid=948%2E294397%2ER7APPY)**
Thanks for being in this fight with us.
— Tammy
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_jct_footer?refcode=em20251227-948&t=25&refcode2=948_294397_R7APPY&akid=948%2E294397%2ER7APPY)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=26&akid=948%2E294397%2ER7APPY)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=27&akid=948%2E294397%2ER7APPY)

Email us: [info@chrisforjustice.com](info@chrisforjustice.com)

PO Box 1921
Madison, WI 53701

Meet Coach Tuberville:

**Coach Tuberville was born in Camden, Arkansas, the third of three children of Olive and Charles Tuberville. College Football 's 2004 Coach of the Year and two-time SEC Coach of the Year, Tommy Tuberville is most often recognized for his success on the field. But it is the legacy of his father, a highly decorated WWII veteran and Purple Heart recipient, that motivates Coach Tuberville to give back to the brave men and women of the United States Armed Forces.

In 1989 Coach Tuberville met his future wife, Suzanne Fette, at the Sugar Bowl in New Orleans. They were married in 1991 and have two boys, Tucker and Troy, who continue to make their dad proud.

In his career as a College Football Coach, Tommy started a long and successful career at the University of Miami, starting as a graduate assistant and rising to the Hurricanes' defensive coordinator. He led the Canes to three AP national championships and coached future Hall of Famers Ray Lewis and Warren Sapp, as well as Dwayne "The Rock" Johnson.

In 1998, Coach Tuberville was announced as the next head coach for the Auburn Tigers. Tuberville's time at Auburn was a success by any measure, as the Tigers captured eight consecutive bowl appearances, including five New Year's Day bowl appearances, five SEC Western Division titles, one SEC Championship, and a 13-0 season in 2004, after which Coach Tuberville was once again named the AP's SEC Coach of the Year. For his efforts during the perfect 2004 season, Tuberville would be named Paul "Bear" Bryant Coach of the Year, Associated Press Coach of the Year, Walter Camp Coach of the Year, Sporting News Coach of the Year, and AFCA Coach of the Year, among others.

_In 2020, Coach Tuberville became Senator Tuberville, winning his election to represent all Alabamians in the United States Senate with the full support of President Trump. Since then, he's supported President Trump's America-First policies while fighting off the Radical Left. In his first term as Senator, Tommy was one of eight Republicans to vote against certifying the 2020 election, has been a strong pro-life advocate, and called out Democrats for being pro-crime.

Now, Coach Tuberville is running for Governor so he can continue representing Alabamians and Americans in his homestate of Alabama. The Radical Left thinks they can flip Alabama blue and turn it into the California of the South. Can he count on your support? _
